monster.name: Alpengrendel

trait.name: Aversion to Faith
trait.text: The alpengrendel has disadvantage on attack rolls while in a consecrated area or on attack rolls against a creature holding or wearing a holy symbol.

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The alpengrendel regains 10 hit points at the start of each of its turns. If the alpengrendel takes acid or lightning damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. The alpengrendel dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The alpengrendel makes one Claws attack and one Tusks attack.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) cold damage.

action.name: Tusks
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) piercing damage plus 5 (1d10) cold damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Frost Breath (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The alpengrendel breathes out a 30-foot cone of frost. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 21 (6d6) cold damage, and until the start of the alpengrendel's next turn, its speed is halved, and it has disadvantage on attack rolls. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

monster.description:
Brute Born of Mountain Ice
Alpengrendel are hulking creatures covered in bluish white fur, with great tusks and horns. They were born of the ice and darkness of their mountaintop homes, shaped by the unseen hands of a deity of cold stone and gloam. The bitter cold of the mountain peaks infuses both their touch and their breath. Alpengrendel draw upon the timelessness of the mountains, healing from otherwise lethal wounds in the blink of an eye. They fear only the power of lightning, which can slay them, and the power of the gods, whom they bitterly resent.
Secret. Their disdain for deities runs so deep that alpengrendel are enraged and unnerved by the trappings of faith.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 487 (Third Party)

monster.name: Bat Hound
monster.languages: Common plus one otherlanguage but can't speak

trait.name: Barred by Water
trait.text: The bat hound can't willingly move into running water or to a space up to 20 feet above running water. If it starts its turn in running water, it takes 10 acid damage and is frightened until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Glide
trait.text: If it isn't incapacitated and isn't wearing heavy armor, the bat hound can extend its membranous wings to move up to 5 feet horizontally for every 1 foot it descends in the air.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The bat hound can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, the bat hound regains a number of hit points equal to the necrotic damage taken, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 13).

action.name: Outmaneuver (Bonus Action)
action.text: The bat hound takes the Dash and Disengage actions, and until the end of the turn, needn't spend extra movement to move a creature it is grappling.

reaction.name: Safe Descent
reaction.text: When the bat hound takes damage from a fall and isn't wearing heavy armor, it extends its wings and reduces the fall's damage to 0.

monster.description:
Bloodthirsty Hunter of the Night
Bat hounds are created through blood magic rituals that manipulate the curse of vampirism to transform a hound into a relentless stalker. Unswervingly loyal companions, bat hounds aid vampiric masters in tracking prey, able to outrun horses and their riders once they smell blood. They seek out stragglers or quarry that are escaping during a hunt and drag them back for capture.
Secret. Running water keeps bat hounds at bay and can destroy them, a lingering remnant from the vampiric curse that spawned them.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 488 (Third Party)

monster.name: Bayou Beast
monster.conditionImmune: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed,poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious

trait.name: Spirits Susceptibility
trait.text: The elemental takes 9 (2d8) force damage for every bottle of alcohol splashed onto it, and its speed is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The elemental makes two Hexed Slam attacks.

action.name: Hexed Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be cursed until the start of the elemental's next turn. While cursed, the creature subtracts 1d4 from its attack rolls and saving throws.

action.name: Swamp Grasp (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: Swamp water and plants roil on the ground or a liquid surface within 20 feet of the elemental. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or take 11 (2d10) poison damage, and the target is restrained for 1 minute, until the elemental becomes incapacitated, or until the elemental dies. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can take an action to make a DC 16 Strength (Athletics) check, ending the restrained condition on the target on a success.

monster.description:
Spirit Hex-Bound to the Swamp
Bayou beasts are elemental spirits bound into great mounds of swamp plant matter through jinxcraft magic. These hulks obey their creators unfailingly, often lingering long after their death and carrying out their final orders. The hexed seals, fashioned from carved trinkets and masks, that trap the spirit in its form impart their curse on the creature's enemies.
Secret. The jinxcraft seals can be washed away with alcohol, hindering and even destroying the bayou beast.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 489 (Third Party)

monster.name: Boneflayer
monster.languages: Sylvan; understands Common but can't speak it

trait.name: Flyby
trait.text: The boneflayer doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Thunderous Ward
trait.text: When the boneflayer hears a drum or gong, or takes thunder damage, it has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The boneflayer makes one Beak attack and one Talons attack.

action.name: Beak
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) slashing damage, and if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Talons
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 14) from both talons. While grappled, the target is restrained.

action.name: Swoop (Recharge 5-6) (Bonus Action)
action.text: If the boneflayer has a creature grappled, the boneflayer flies up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks and drops that creature.

monster.description:
Voracious Carrion Eater
Boneflayers are cruel, vulture-like creatures that originated from a broken fey pact. The creatures aren't content to wait for something to die and instead make their own carrion to gorge upon—they delight in snatching prey in their talons, carrying it into the air, and dropping it to its death. Boneflayers earn their name by using their sizeable, filth-riddled beaks to cleave flesh from bone and hoard the meat until it ages enough for them to enjoy. Cults sometimes use boneflayer killing ground as a source of bones to burn in ritual bonfires, occasionally making pacts with the creatures to pay or trade for the creature's castoffs. Villages that fear boneflayer attacks ward them off with thundering drums or gongs whenever they circle overhead.
Secret. A lingering result of the fey bargain that created them, boneflayers are disoriented and diminished by loud, thunderous noises.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 490 (Third Party)

monster.name: Boogleswarm
monster.languages: understands Common but can only speak ifthe swarm has gathered at least one Humanoid tooth
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, frightened, grappled,paralyzed, petrified, prone, restrained, stunned

trait.name: Mimicry (Only if the Swarm Can Speak)
trait.text: The swarm can speak in the voice of a creature whose tooth it has implanted (see the Bite action). Listeners must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom (Insight) check to discern the mimicry.

trait.name: Swarm
trait.text: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny weasel. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Sweet Tooth
trait.text: If the swarm can see or smell sugar at the start of its turn, it must use all its movement to move closer to the sugar. If it reaches the sugar, the swarm becomes incapacitated until the start of its next turn as it consumes the sugar, and this trait doesn't function again until the swarm finishes a short or long rest.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The swarm makes two Bite attacks that must have different targets.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (4d4) piercing damage, or 5 (2d4) piercing damage if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer. If the target is a creature that has teeth, it must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or lose one of its teeth. The swarm immediately implants the tooth into one of its mouths. A creature that has already lost a tooth to this attack automatically succeeds on the saving throw.

monster.description:
Tricksy Fey Weasels
Boogleswarms are mischievous Fey spirits that take the form of weasels gathered into a slinking mob. These spirits covet and collect teeth of all kinds, but they favor the teeth of Humanoids most of all. Tales of tooth faeries might have their origin in boogleswarms, as they are known to enter into agreements to trade for teeth—such as those lost by children and tucked under a pillow—for rewards, favors, or safety.
Secret. Boogleswarms go wild for sugar, whether pure or in heavily-sweetened foods like candy, and are compelled to take and consume any they find.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 491 (Third Party)

monster.name: Centipede Hag

trait.name: Coven Magic
trait.text: While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability: Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Lucky Charm (lasts until the hag casts it again, or chooses to end it with no action required), or Unseen Servant. The hag must finish a long rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

trait.name: Scoured Filth
trait.text: The hag is burned by soapy water. Treat a bucket or flask of it splashed on the hag as a vial of acid. If the hag is fully immersed in soapy water at the start of its turn, it takes 20 acid damage.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The hag can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The hag makes two Bite attacks or uses Spellcasting to cast Contagion. It can also use Warping Caress.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the start of the hag's next turn.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17):
• At Will: Contagion, Disguise Self (24-hour duration), Hold Person, Invisibility (self only) • 1/day Each: Insect Plague (as 8th-level spell), Polymorph

action.name: Warping Caress
action.text: The hag touches a creature and warps its flesh. The target must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 19 (4d6 + 5) necrotic damage and gains 1 level of exhaustion. Until the target's exhaustion level is reduced to 0, its body is warped and twisted, and it has disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws that use Strength or Dexterity. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. If a Humanoid dies from this action, the hag can spend 2 days of work to shape its corpse into a Deepdreck, or it can combine it with the corpse of a horse to create a Fleshdobbin.

monster.spells: Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Lucky Charm (TP), Unseen Servant, Contagion, Disguise Self, Hold Person, Invisibility, Insect Plague, Polymorph
monster.description:
Hag of Pestilence and Flesh-Shaping
Centipede hags are grotesque witches that revel in disease and filth, filling their presence with the scuttling vermin they adore. Their magic thrives in the corruption and warping of bodies, and they often engage in horrifying experimentation in grafting, swapping, and combining body parts to create nightmarish new creatures.
Secret. Centipede hags draw more than strength from the filth that covers them; it is the essence of their being. Scrubbing it away dissolves the hag.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 492 (Third Party)

monster.name: Corvodaemon

trait.name: Ashen Forbiddance
trait.text: The corvodaemon can't cross a line of ashes created by burning feathers sprinkled with Holy Water.

trait.name: Demonic Restoration
trait.text: If the corvodaemon dies outside the Abyss, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its hit points somewhere in the Abyss.

trait.name: Devour Anima
trait.text: A creature the corvodaemon reduced to 0 hit points is cursed until it regains any hit points. When the cursed creature fails a death saving throw at the start of its turn, it dies regardless of how many failures it has.

trait.name: Mimicry
trait.text: The corvodaemon can mimic the voice of any creature it has heard, or of a creature known to one of the creatures within range of its telepathy. A hearer can discern the voices are imitations with a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Insight) check.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The corvodaemon can use Feeding Frenzy if available. It then makes one Beak attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Beak
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage.

action.name: Claws
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (2d4 + 3) slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Feeding Frenzy (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The corvodaemon enters a voracious feeding firenzy, driven by its insatiable hunger for souls. Until the start of its next turn, the corvodaemon's speed doubles, and when it damages a creature, it gains temporary hit points equal to half the damage taken.

monster.description:
Demon of Hunger and Secrets
Resembling a monstrous crow, the corvodaemon's sleek and twisted form soars on elongated wings tipped with razor-sharp, finger-like claws, its six amber eyes constantly seeking for prey. Insatiable hunger for both flesh and souls—along with their accumulated secrets—gnaws at these Fiends. Their mimicry lets them take on the voice of those known and trusted by their would-be meals, luring the hapless mortals to their doom.
Secret. Savvy demon hunters bar corvodaemons with warding lines of ash prepared from feathers.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 493 (Third Party)

monster.name: Crimson Clergy

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The clergy can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Vampire Weakness
trait.text: The clergy has the following weaknesses:

trait.name: Self-Loathing
trait.text: The clergy has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws if it can see its reflection.

trait.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
trait.text: The clergy takes 20 radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Consume
action.text: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (2d6 + 3) necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken until it finishes a long rest, and the clergy regains hit points equal to that amount.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The clergy casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13):
• At Will: Disguise Self, Thaumaturgy

action.name: Crimson Blessing (1/Day) (Bonus Action)
action.text: The clergy casts Bless using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

monster.description:
Vampiric Zealot
Crimson clergy are Undead cultists, often created, led, and taught by a more powerful vampire. Crimson clergy view the consumption of blood—and in its use during their rituals and when casting spells—not only as necessary but sacred. Their hunger reaches beyond the limits of their fangs and claws, calling to the blood of their victims and drawing it out in scarlet ribbons.
Secret. Crimson clergy struggle to accept how monstrous they have become. They can't stand the sight of themselves and see their true visage in their reflection.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 494 (Third Party)

monster.name: Crooked Effigy

trait.name: Crooked Thorns
trait.text: At the start of each of its turns, the effigy deals 5 (1d10) psychic damage to any creature it is grappling or any creature grappling it.

trait.name: Old Bindings
trait.text: The effigy takes an extra 10 force damage if it's hit by an attack roll using smith's tools or tinker's tools (treat as a club or light hammer).

action.name: Impale
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 13) from one of two branch arms. Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained.

monster.description:
Splinter of Crooked Power
Crooked effigies are servants of a twisted archfey known as the Crooked Queen. They are made of gnarled wood and show some bipedal and feminine features in their queen's image. Crooked effigies impale their enemies with their branch-like arms and drag foes in close, where the thorns on their bodies carry the sting of a victim's own corruption.
Secret. Crooked effigies are bound to the Old Ways, and they recoil from the tools of forging and tinkering symbols of a growing civilization.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 495 (Third Party)

monster.name: Crooked Man

trait.name: Aversion to Self-Sacrifice
trait.text: The crooked man takes 20 necrotic damage if he can see a creature willingly take damage or gain a condition in place of someone else, or to someone else's benefit.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The crooked man has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The crooked man makes two Crooked Claw attacks, and he can replace one attack with a use of Snap Limb. He can also use Confusion if available.

action.name: Crooked Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) slashing damage plus 21 (6d6) psychic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 16) from one of two claws. Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained.

action.name: Snap Limb
action.text:
One creature the crooked man is grappling must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw. A creature without limbs that can break is immune to this effect. On a failed save, the target takes 35 (10d6) force damage, and the bones (or equivalent) in one of its limbs (crooked man's choice) breaks, causing an appropriate effect below. A broken limb heals in 40 days, or when the creature regains any hit points from magic or Regeneration. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
• Broken Arm. Disadvantage on attack rolls. Can't hold anything or manipulate objects with the limb.
• Broken Leg. Speed halved. If the creature takes the Dash action, it must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.
• Broken Wing. Flying speed reduced to 0

action.name: Confusion (1/Day)
action.text: The crooked man casts Confusion, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17).

action.name: Blinking Teleport (Bonus Action)
action.text: The crooked man becomes invisible for 1 minute and teleports to an unoccupied space he can see within 60 feet. The condition ends immediately after the crooked man deals any damage.

monster.description:
Broken Spirit of Betrayal
The crooked man is an Undead embodiment of broken trust and the twisting of purpose. Those who die having betrayed what they hold sacred, embracing their treachery to their core, might return in this broken, gnarled form of snapped bones with a crooked grin. The creature's fingers end in curling claws that hook and hold, and the duplicity in its heart bleeds out to twist allies against one another. Despite the creature's name, anyone can become this manifestation of betrayal.
Secret. The crooked man can't abide self-sacrifice and withers in its presence.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 496 (Third Party)

monster.name: Crowstorm
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrified, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned

trait.name: False Crows
trait.text: The swarm can't enter a space with a drawn or painted image or crafted effigy of a crow. A creature holding such an icon has advantage on saving throws the swarm forces it to make.

trait.name: Swarm
trait.text: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny crow. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Wing Bind
trait.text: Each creature (other than a Fiend) with a flying speed that starts its turn within 30 feet of the swarm must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or have its flying speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Peck
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d8 + 3) piercing damage, or 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer. If the target is a creature, it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of the swarm's next turn.

action.name: Cacophony (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: Each creature within 30 feet of the swarm must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage and is frightened until the start of the swarm's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

monster.description:
Fiendish Murder
A crowstorm is a great, swirling mass of dark feathers accompanied constant, harshly malevolent crow calls. Hundreds of fiendish birds fly in unison, their beady eyes dripping blood as they swoop down to tear at the flesh of their victims. A crowstorm's horrific caws mingle into an eerie cacophony of pleading whispers, and it robs the gift of flight from anything that draws too close.
Secret. While they might take the form of crows or possess crow bodies, crowstorms are held at bay by drawn images or crafted effigies of crows.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 497 (Third Party)

monster.name: Deepdreck

trait.name: Explosive Death
trait.text: When the deepdreck dies, it explodes, and each creature within 15 feet of it must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking 13 (3d8) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Fragrance Aversion
trait.text: The deepdreck has disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws while within 10 feet of burning incense or a creature wearing Perfume.

trait.name: Tunneler
trait.text: The deepdreck can burrow through solid rock at half its burrowing speed and leaves a 5-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The deepdreck makes two attacks, using Claw or Putrid Emission in any combination.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage.

action.name: Putrid Emission
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) poison damage, and the target is poisoned until the start of the deepdreck's next turn.

monster.description:
Poisoned Subterranean Wretch
Deepdrecks were miners long ago who found themselves far too deep beneath the earth, where they were cursed and corrupted by deforming powers. Now they tunnel and dig greedily, bloated with poisonous gases. Astute observers have noted that these monsters occasionally bear a resemblance to missing loved ones who have long been thought dead.
Secret. The toxic essence of deepdrecks is disrupted by pleasant scents such as flowers, perfume, spices, or incense.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 498 (Third Party)

monster.name: Drakkenhob

trait.name: Irresistible Payment
trait.text: As an action, a creature that offers the drakkenhob alcohol or any amount of gold and succeeds on a DC 16 Charisma (Persuasion) check forces the drakkenhob to accept the offer and immediately use its reaction to move up to half its speed to get away from the creature by the safest route possible.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The drakkenhob makes two Rend attacks.

action.name: Rend
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) piercing damage plus 11 (2d10) psychic damage.

action.name: Terrifying Breath (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The drakkenhob breathes waves of panic in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 14 (4d6) psychic damage, and it drops whatever it is holding and becomes frightened for 1 minute. A frightened target takes the Dash action and moves away from the drakkenhob by the safest route on each of its turns unless there's nowhere to move. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A frightened creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Call the Hounds (Bonus Action)
action.text: The drakkenhob lets out a wolf-like howl and magically summons three hunting hounds (use the Wolf stat block) that appear in unoccupied spaces within 60 feet of it. The hounds take their turn immediately after the drakkenhob on the same initiative count, and they obey the drakkenhob's mental commands. The hounds vanish when they or the drakkenhob drop to 0 hit points, or when the drakkenhob uses this bonus action again.

monster.description:
Dragon Disguised as a Festival Horse
The drakkenhob is a draconic monster with a sinister disguise, presenting itself as an actor wearing a horse costume, such as a mounted knight or a festively clad rider. Its draconic head resembles a decorated or armored horse's head, and its sharp teeth ft together to appear like blunt, harmless decorations. The drakkenhob uses this benign guise to get close to its prey, which are typically within crowds near festivals or parades, before it summons its similarly garbed hunting hounds and strikes. Its breath is a blood-curdling wave of terror that makes lesser victims drop dead from fright alone.
Secret. Unable to resist revelry and merriment, drakkenhob are driven away by payment offered in gold or alcohol.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 499 (Third Party)

monster.name: Dream Eater

trait.name: Flyby
trait.text: The dream eater doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Moth Ward
trait.text: A creature holding a moth's wing automatically succeeds on saving throws against the dream eater's Hypnotic Wings.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The dream eater can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The dream eater makes two Talons attacks and can use Devour Dreams.

action.name: Talons
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 14) from one of two talons, and it is restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Devour Dreams
action.text: The dream eater unfurls its tongue and devours the dreams of a creature it is grappling, or an incapacitated creature within 5 feet of it. The target must make a DC 16 Charisma saving throw. Incapacitated targets fail the save automatically. On a failed save, the target takes 18 (4d8) psychic damage, and its Charisma score decreases by 1d6. A creature reduced to 0 Charisma by this effect becomes incapacitated until its mind is restored with a Greater Restoration, Heal, or Wish spell. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until it finishes a long rest. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Hypnotic Wings (Bonus Action)
action.text: The dream eater's wings shine with dazzling rainbow colors until the start of its next turn, emitting bright light in a 30-foot radius from the dream eater and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Each other creature within the bright light that can see the dream eater must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be incapacitated until the start of the dream eater's next turn. While incapacitated in this way, the target's speed is reduced to 0.

monster.description:
Devourer of Nightmares
Dream eaters are horrors that feed on the dreams, sense of identity, and—some say—the very souls of other creatures. Dream eaters have the bodies and wings of great owls, while their beaks are filled with blunt, humanlike teeth and hide long snaking tongues. Their feathers are drab colors on the exterior, but their wings display a dazzling cascade of rainbow hues on the undersides when unfurled. These colors flow and shift, mesmerizing the dream eater's prey and leaving them helpless. A creature whose dreams are devoured is left in a listless, near-blank state. Without help, their victims waste away.
Secret. Dream eaters are confused by the intricate patterns on moth wings, and it disrupts their own colorful hypnosis.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 500 (Third Party)

monster.name: Ferrymen

trait.name: Coffin Boat
trait.text: The ferryman rides an open coffin on a swell of swamp water, allowing it to travel with a speed of 40 feet over any surface, including liquids, ignoring difficult terrain. The coffin can hold two other Medium creatures. Two Small creatures or four Tiny creatures count as one Medium creature. While riding the coffin the ferryman has immunity to being knocked prone, and the coffin moves with the ferryman unless the ferryman becomes incapacitated. The coffin has AC 15; HP 25; immunity to necrotic, poison, and psychic damage. If the coffin is destroyed, the ferryman can magically create a new one using a 10-minute ritual.

trait.name: Payment
trait.text: The ferryman must peacefully offer a ride in its boat to a creature that takes an action to offer it 3 cp. If that creature is cursed by the ferryman, the curse ends. If the boat is full, the ferryman must return to offer the creature a ride once its current passengers leave the boat. If the creature or its allies damage the ferryman or force it to make a saving throw, this effect ends and the creature can't use that ferryman's Payment trait again.

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The ferryman regains 10 hit points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 hit point.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The ferryman makes two Lantern Pole attacks and uses Hexed Lantern if available

action.name: Lantern Pole
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Hexed Lantern (1/Day)
action.text: The ferryman unleashes a burst of light from its lantern in a 30-foot cone. Each creature of the ferryman's choice in that area must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be cursed for 1 minute. While cursed in this way, the target must subtract 1d4 from ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws.

monster.description:
Cursed Undead Boatman
Ferrymen are Undead created through the use of curses and jinxcraft and are doomed to ply the waters where they were raised. They pole through the bayous and rivers, and even across land on a wave of swamp water, in coffin boats. They can focus the malice of their curse through the light of their lantern poles, jinxing enemies or those who try to force a ride without payment.
Secret. Paying a ferryman 3 copper coins lifts any curse the creature placed on the payer, and it entitles one to a single unnerving but safe ride in the ferryman's coffin boat.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 501 (Third Party)

monster.name: Festerhulk

trait.name: Fungal Step
trait.text: The festerhulk ignores difficult terrain caused by plants or fungi.

trait.name: Spore Cloud
trait.text: At the end of each of the festerhulk's turns, each creature within 5 feet of the festerhulk takes 10 (3d6) necrotic damage and is poisoned until the end of its next turn. A Giant or Humanoid killed by this damage rises as a new creature 24 hours later: Festerhulk (if a Giant), Zombie (if a Humanoid)

trait.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
trait.text: The festerhulk takes 10 radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The festerhulk makes two Fetid Slam attacks and uses Slime Mold if available.

action.name: Fetid Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (2d12 + 7) bludgeoning damage plus 13 (3d8) necrotic damage.

action.name: Slime Mold (1/Day)
action.text: The festerhulk disgorges a mass of ravenous slime mold into an unoccupied space within 20 feet. This mass uses the Black Pudding stat block except it is a Plant, and its size is Medium. The mass acts on the festerhulk's initiative. It, and any masses it Split into, dries up and dies in 1 hour.

monster.description:
Fungal Undead Giant
Festerhulks are dead giants whose corpses are infested with fungus engorged by the magic of decay. This fungus has consumed and replaced much of the giant's flesh and now animates the corpse. The heads of these giants are often completely overtaken by repulsive mushrooms and other fungal growths. The spores given of by their infestation can spread to victims killed by festerhulks, giving rise to further Undead.
Secret. Festerhulks shrink from sunlight, which harms the fungus that animates them.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 502 (Third Party)

monster.name: Fleshdobbin

trait.name: Bridle Binding
trait.text: If a creature puts a bridle on the fleshdobbin (treat as securing manacles), the fleshdobbin is charmed while it wears the bridle, and it can't take the bridle of on its own.

trait.name: Immutable Form
trait.text: The fleshdobbin is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The fleshdobbin makes one Claw attack and one Wailing Halberd attack.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d6 + 5) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked prone.

action.name: Wailing Halberd
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (1d10 + 5) slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) psychic damage.

action.name: Shriek (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The fleshdobbin shrieks in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 28 (8d6) psychic damage and is frightened for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A frightened creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Parry
reaction.text: When a creature the fleshdobbin can see hits it with a melee attack roll while the fleshdobbin is wielding a Melee weapon, the fleshdobbin adds 3 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

monster.description:
Flesh-Crafted Amalgam of Knight and Horse
Fleshdobbins are abominations created when a horse and its rider are horrifically melded through fleshcrafting magic. Their limbs stretch and deform, though afterward, their bodies resist further transformation. Fitted with saddles and bearing savage weaponry, these monsters may look like riderless horses from a distance, but each leg culminates in oddly human-looking gnarled hands with fingers ending in wicked claws. Their voices are the comingled shrieks of human and horse.
Secret. Affixing a bridle to a fleshdobbin causes the instincts of the horse within the amalgam to take over, becoming a loyal steed once more.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 503 (Third Party)

monster.name: Gloomwood

trait.name: Blighted Aura
trait.text: The ground within 20 feet of the gloomwood is difficult terrain from mushrooms and fungus that grow in the area. A creature that ends its turn in the area takes 5 (1d10) necrotic damage.

trait.name: Carved Past
trait.text: As an action, a creature can use a dagger or woodcarver's tools to carve the name in Sylvan of the ancient home of the gloomwoods into the gloomwood's trunk. Until the gloomwood finishes a long rest, it can't use its Fungal Fling or Spore Stomp actions.

trait.name: Fungal Step
trait.text: The gloomwood ignores difficult terrain caused by plants or fungi.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The gloomwood makes two attacks, using Slam or Fungal Fling in any combination

action.name: Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 16) from one of two branches. Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained.

action.name: Fungal Fling
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (5d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Spore Stomp (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) bludgeoning damage. Hit or Miss: Each creature within a 20-foot sphere centered on the target must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 36 (8d8) poison damage and is poisoned until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

monster.description:
Blighted Treant
Gloomwoods were once treants in service to an archfey, but they were corrupted by a supernatural fungal blight. The growth bores through their trunks and into their souls, making the gloomwoods cruel and vicious, interested only in spreading more decay. Fungal spores drift from their trunks and branches, rotting any flesh and digging into the ground around them to spread the blight.
Secret. Carving the Sylvan name of the forest the gloomwood once called home into the creature's trunk quells its aggression.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 504 (Third Party)

monster.name: Gobblegeist

trait.name: Flyby
trait.text: The gobblegeist doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Repelled by Stars
trait.text: The gobblegeist can't willingly move to a space within 10 feet of a seven-pointed star drawn on a surface or woven from wicker.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The gobblegeist makes two Beak attacks. It can replace one attack with a Talons attack.

action.name: Beak
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken until it finishes a long rest, and the gobblegeist regains hit points equal to that amount.

action.name: Talons
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 15) from both talons, and it is restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Eye Beam
action.text: The gobblegeist unleashes a beam from its eye in a line that is 120 feet long, and 5 feet wide. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw or take 6 (1d12) psychic damage and become stunned until the start of the gobblegeist's next turn.

monster.description:
Blood-Drinking Avian Predator
Gobblegeists are massive birdlike monsters with earth-toned plumage, reptilian faces and claws, and a metallic beak edged with sharp teeth. These ravenous creatures descend from forest canopies or hilltops to snatch their prey unawares and carry them of to feed on their blood in solitude. Their single, unblinking eye can unnerve and dazzle more alert prey, making would-be threats an easy meal.
Secret. The folk who venerate the Old Ways believe that a seven-pointed star repels gobblegeists. They keep the monsters at bay by painting or weaving star sigils from wicker to hang from their homes or in trees.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 505 (Third Party)

monster.name: Heralds of Fools

trait.name: Endless Footwork
trait.text: When the herald's speed is 0, attack rolls made against it have advantage, and if its speed is 0 at the start of its turn, it takes 20 necrotic damage.

trait.name: Perfect Grace
trait.text: The herald doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it leaves an enemy's reach.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The herald makes three Banner Pole attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Lethal Jape.

action.name: Banner Pole
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit (with advantage if the target's speed is 0), reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d10 + 6) slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) thunder damage, and if the target is a Large or smaller creature, the herald can move it to any other unoccupied space within the Banner Pole's reach.

action.name: Lethal Jape
action.text: The herald taunts one creature it can see within 60 feet of itself. The target must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 24 (7d6) psychic damage, and its speed is 0 until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

reaction.name: Wave the Banner
reaction.text: When an ally of the herald's within 60 feet of it misses with an attack roll or fails a saving throw, the ally can reroll the d20 and must use the new roll.

monster.description:
Leader of Fiendish Revels
Heralds of fools lead troupes of fiendish revelers in service to the enigmatic Lord of Fools, a clown or jester entity who is a curious mix of levity and cruelty (though always with a sense of irony). The heralds are agile and graceful, every movement a dance, spinning their banner poles in intricate displays that can swiftly turn from entertainment to death.
Secret. Heralds of fools are wonderful dancers, and they must never stop dancing, or they quickly perish.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 506 (Third Party)

monster.name: Hospital Horror
monster.languages: understands Common and Deep Speech but can't speak

trait.name: Aversion to Needles
trait.text: If the horror takes piercing damage, it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
trait.text: The horror has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the horror's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The horror makes two Graft Blade attacks.

action.name: Graft Blade
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage.

action.name: Stim Burst (Bonus Action)
action.text: The horror takes the Dash action and can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.

monster.description:
Mutated Experiment
Hospital horrors were once people, but now they are repulsive mockeries created through surgical and magical alteration. They have no memory of their past lives, existing only within a chemically induced rage. Often, they are conditioned by the use of specific command phrases that allow them to be controlled.
Secret. Hospital horrors have spent much of their existence being prodded with needles, so being pierced frays what few wits they retain.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 507 (Third Party)

monster.name: House of Horror
monster.languages: understands Common plus one other language but can't speak
monster.conditionImmune: exhaustion, grappled, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained

trait.name: Construction
trait.text: Creatures can enter the house's space and navigate it as a normal building. The house makes attacks from any surface or object that isn't being worn or carried within its space. It doesn't have disadvantage on ranged attack rolls due to an enemy being within 5 feet of it. If a creature deals 30 or more damage in a single turn to a 5-foot section of the house's structure, the house must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or a 5-foot opening appears in that space until the start of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Exorcism Susceptibility
trait.text: If the house is targeted by a Dispel Evil and Good spell, it becomes stunned. At the end of each of its turns, it can make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw, ending the condition on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. As an action, a creature inside the house that throws a fask of holy water against the house and succeeds on a DC 20 Charisma check affects the house as above.

trait.name: Soul Trap
trait.text: A Humanoid that dies within the house has its soul trapped there until the house dies. The creature can't be returned to life by any means short of a True Resurrection or Wish spell.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The house makes three attacks, using Structure Slam or Object Fling in any combination, and it uses Agonized Souls

action.name: Structure Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) force damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 16). The house can grapple up to two creatures at a time, using animated furniture, objects, or fixtures such as stair railings.

action.name: Object Fling
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d4 + 6) bludgeoning or piercing damage (house's choice). If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is knocked prone.

action.name: Agonized Souls
action.text: The house's stolen souls torment a creature within 10 feet of the house. The target must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw, taking 36 (8d8) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Alter Structure (Bonus Action)
action.text: The house can reshape itself to change the location of walls, doors, floors, and other features of its construction. When one of these features appears or disappears, a creature within 5 feet of a changing feature can make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. Ona successful save, the creature chooses which side of a feature it ends up on, or it moves to the nearest unoccupied space to avoid falling.

monster.description:
Cursed House Brought to Malevolent Life
Houses of horror are living houses, often angry or bitter due to the circumstances of their awakening. They were once mundane buildings or dwellings where powerful magics of life and death took place and infused the stones and timber. Houses of horror jealously covet the true life that its former inhabitants enjoyed, or they carry echoes of the suffering that bodies and souls experienced within them. The houses vent this fury on any who enter, tormenting and terrifying trespassers. The souls of those who die inside are trapped, waiting to be unleashed upon future victims.
Secret. While not haunted houses in the traditional sense, houses of horror can have their consciousness quelled by the same exorcism or purification rituals that put unquiet spirits to rest or drive out possessing spirits.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 508 (Third Party)

monster.name: Hypnovulfen

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The hypnovulfen has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The hypnovulfen makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack, and it can use Swallow.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 17), and it is restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d6 + 7) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 20 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Swallow
action.text: The hypnovulfen makes one Bite attack against a Medium or smaller creature it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target takes the bite's damage and is swallowed, and the grapple ends. The swallowed target is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the hypnovulfen, and it takes 21 (6d6) psychic damage at the start of each of the hypnovulfen's turns. The hypnovulfen can only have one creature swallowed at a time. If the creature dies from this damage, the hypnovulfen absorbs its memories and regains 25 hit points. If the hypnovulfen takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from the swallowed creature, the hypnovulfen must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate the creature, which falls prone in a space within 10 feet of the hypnovulfen. If the hypnovulfen dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained by it and can escape from the corpse by using10 feet of movement, exiting prone.

action.name: Hypnotic Eyes (Recharge 5-6) (Bonus Action)
action.text: The hypnovulfen hypnotizes anyone in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 19 Wisdom saving throw. A creature holding a keepsake from a loved one succeeds automatically. On a failed save, the creature is charmed for 1 minute or until it takes damage. While charmed in this way, it is incapacitated and must move as close to the hypnovulfen as possible by the safest route. The creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

monster.description:
Hungry Hypnotizing Wolf
Hypnovulfen resemble massive wolves with unnaturally large orange eyes and wide, crooked maws. Despite their size, hypnovulfen are shockingly stealthy, able to stalk their prey and snare them with a hypnotic gaze from the shadows. The gaze beguiles victims into seeing a loved one beckoning them closer for a warm embrace. All the better for a hypnovulfen to feed and consume the unfortunate morsel's memories.
Secret. Hypnovulfen have no power to mesmerize anyone holding a keepsake or memento received from a loved one, as it overpowers the false beckoning of the monster's gaze.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 509 (Third Party)

monster.name: Invader out of Space

trait.name: Alien Mind
trait.text: If a creature tries to read the intruder's mind or deals psychic damage to it, that creature must succeed on a DC 16 Intelligence saving throw or be stunned for 1 minute. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Gliding Levitation
trait.text: The invader hovers 6 inches above the ground and leaves no tracks. It ignores difficult terrain caused by conditions on the ground, can move over liquids, and takes no damage from falling.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The invader has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Weak Constitution
trait.text: The invader has disadvantage on saving throws to avoid or end the poisoned condition. If the invader is poisoned at the start of its turn, it takes 20 necrotic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The invader makes three Disrupting Touch attacks and uses Unnerving Glare if available.

action.name: Disrupting Touch
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) force damage. A creature killed or an object destroyed by this damage is reduced to a fine gray dust. Magic items, and any equipment worn or carried by a disintegrated creature, don't disintegrate.

action.name: Unnerving Glare (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: One creature the invader can see within 60 feet of itself must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be incapacitated until the start of the invader's next turn.

reaction.name: Otherwordly Slip
reaction.text: When the invader takes damage, it teleports to an unoccupied space it can see within 60 feet of itself, then each creature within 30 feet of the space where the invader vanished or appeared (invader's choice) must make a DC 16 Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 16 (3d10) psychic damage, and its speed is halved until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

monster.description:
Intruder From Beyond the Stars
Invaders out of space appeared after a falling star blazed down from the cosmos. These alien beings have an unsettling habit of appearing between glances and silently watching. Invaders stand upright like a biped, shrouded in what are either voluminous robes or draping folds of skin. Observers who are lucky find the invaders gone in a blink, and those who aren't so fortunate hear the bloodcurdling hiss these things from beyond the stars emit as they attack. All that remains of their victims is a spray of gray powder amid crumpled clothing.
Secret. The invaders have little resilience to the toxic and virulent threats of the Material Plane, and they succumb quickly to diseases and poisons.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 510 (Third Party)

monster.name: Kackle
monster.resist: acid, cold, fire, lightning, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained

trait.name: Flickering Defense
trait.text: The kackle adds twice its Dexterity modifier to its AC.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The kackle can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Lantern Vulnerability
trait.text: The kackle's lantern has AC 15 and immunity to poison and psychic damage. Any damage the lantern takes deals half that amount of force damage to the kackle.

action.name: Lantern Flame
action.text: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 90 ft., one target. Hit: Varies by lantern color (kackle's choice):

action.name: Blue
action.text: 7 (1d6 + 4) cold damage, and the target is restrained until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Green
action.text: 20 (3d10 + 4) necrotic damage, and the target's speed is 0 until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Purple
action.text: 10 (1d12 + 4) psychic damage, and the target is frightened until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Red
action.text: 15 (2d10 + 4) fire damage, and if the target is a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature within 5 feet of the target takes an action to douse the flames, the target takes 5 (1d10) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Flickering Teleport (Bonus Action)
action.text: The kackle teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. The kackle can then change its lantern color, and each creature within 5 feet of the kackle's destination space must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 13 (3d8) damage of the same type as the current Lantern Flame color on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

monster.description:
Laughing Lantern Bearer
Those who were particularly sadistic in life and died consumed by wicked mirth linger as Kackles—ghostly figures that drift above the ground, always fitting just out of sight. Adorned in tattered cloaks and concealing hoods or hats, their hollow laughter echoes through dark places, fading in and out to confuse and terrify their victims. Kackles always carry lanterns lit with ghostly flames, the color of which changes to Ω a kackle's current cruelty of choice.
Secret. Kackles are bound to their lanterns, and smashing their lights can snuff out the spirits existence.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 511 (Third Party)

monster.name: Keeper of the Blight

trait.name: Blight Dependent
trait.text: If the keeper is targeted by an effect that ends the poisoned condition, it takes 10 (3d6) force damage and has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Fungal Step
trait.text: The keeper ignores difficult terrain caused by plants or fungi.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The keeper makes two attacks, using Blight Staff or Spore Spray in any combination, and it uses Rotting Ritual.

action.name: Blight Staff
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Spore Spray
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, range 60 ft., one creature. Hit: 9 (2d8) poison damage. The target must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of the keeper's next turn.

action.name: Rotting Ritual
action.text:
The keeper magically creates one of the following effects of its choice. It can't use the same effect twice in a row.
Frenzying Spores. The keeper wafts spores toward a creature the keeper can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage, and it must use its reaction to make a melee or ranged attack against a creature of the keeper's choice that is within reach or range of the attack. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
Blight Spawn. The keeper magically summons a Rotweaver (lacks a Hunting Horn) in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of itself. The rotweaver takes its turn immediately after the keeper on the same initiative count, and it obeys the keeper. The rotweaver vanishes after 1 minute, or when it or the keeper dies.
Devouring Fungus. Life-sapping fungus sprouts from the ground in a 60-foot radius centered on the keeper. The area is difficult terrain, and a creature is poisoned while within the area. The fungus remains for 1 minute, until the keeper uses this effect again, or until it dies.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The keeper casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16, +8 to hit with spell attacks):
• At Will: Druidcraft, Ray of Sickness • 1/day: Blight

monster.description:
Druid Transformed by Blight
Keepers of the blight arise from Fey beings that dwell in a natural region that becomes overtaken by a supernatural or spiritual rot. Where they once tended the balance and cycles of their home, keepers are consumed by the blight and channel their magic through fungus, mold, and rot, pushing the cycle of life toward death, decay, and the flourishing of things that thrive in gloom.
Secret. The fungus growing throughout keepers of the blight empowers and sustains them, and killing that growth diminishes their power.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 512 (Third Party)

monster.name: Ketgrinn

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The ketgrinn has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Mimicry
trait.text: The ketgrinn can mimic any voice it has heard, or a voice it has stolen (see Consume Voice below). A creature that hears the voice can tell it's an imitation with a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Insight) check. The DC increases to 19 for a voice the ketgrinn has stolen.

trait.name: Riddle Bound
trait.text: As an action, a creature that poses a riddle to the ketgrinn and succeeds on a DC 16 Charisma (Deception) check incapacitates the ketgrinn until the start of the creature's next turn. Each riddle posed increases the DC for future riddles by 1 for 24 hours.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The ketgrinn makes two Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Consume Voice.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d4 + 4) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage, and if the target is a creature, it has disadvantage on the next saving throw the ketgrinn forces it to make before the end of the ketgrinn's next turn.

action.name: Consume Voice
action.text: The ketgrinn leers menacingly at one creature within 5 feet of it. The target must make a DC 14 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 18 (4d8) psychic damage, and the ketgrinn steals the target's voice. The target can't speak or make any verbal sound while its voice is stolen. The target repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If it fails three of these saving throws, the loss is permanent, and its voice only returns when the ketgrinn dies. On a successful initial save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Invisibility
action.text: The ketgrinn casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

action.name: Teleport (Bonus Action)
action.text: The ketgrinn teleports up to 40 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

monster.description:
Voice-Stealing Trickster
Ketgrinn are catlike Fey spirits who delight in puzzles, riddles, and bargains. They have large, bright eyes and too-wide grins that gleam starkly even in shadows. Ketgrinn are tricksters and spies, often sent by those they serve to steal the voices of a rival's minions to sow chaos and confusion.
Secret. Ketgrinn can't resist a puzzle or riddle, and a difficult one can send a ketgrinn's mind spiraling.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 513 (Third Party)

monster.name: Lake Dredger

trait.name: Depicted Demise
trait.text: The lake dredger takes 20 force damage at the start of its turn if it can see a creature holding a created depiction (such as a drawing, painting, or carving) of the lake dredger.

trait.name: Silt Stealth
trait.text: The lake dredger has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made when in an area with aquatic plants or seaweed.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The lake dredger makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 16), and it is restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Tail
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (4d8 + 6) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it must succeed on a DC 18 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Swallow (Bonus Action)
action.text: The lake dredger makes one Bite attack against a Large or smaller creature it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is swallowed, and the grapple ends. The swallowed target is restrained, it has three-quarters cover against attacks and other effects outside the lake dredger, and it takes 21 (6d6) necrotic damage at the start of each of the lake dredger's turns. The lake dredger can only have one creature swallowed at a time. As an action, the swallowed creature can escape through the lake dredger's ribcage. The creature takes 11 (2d10) piercing damage and emerges in a space within 5 feet of the lake dredger. If the lake dredger dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained by it and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting prone.

monster.description:
Undead Aquatic Monster
Lake dredgers are Undead water behemoths, such as whales, giant fsh, or dinosaurs. These creatures are reanimated through the deep, primal fear of the unknown horrors that lurk in the murky depths. They dwell in the beds of lakes, rivers, or oceans, dredging up the detritus to replenish their ever-rotting mass. They also add to their decaying bulk by feeding on living creatures, especially drawn to those who exhibit fear of the deep. Once prey is devoured, it is impaled on its cracked ribs until the waterlogged flesh becomes one with the beast's.
Secret. Lake dredgers exist because of the fear of the unknown, and their essence is undone in the presence of a captured or crafted depiction of themselves, such as a drawing or carving.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 514 (Third Party)

monster.name: Lurker in the Dark

trait.name: Nightmarish Contortion
trait.text: The lurker can ft into a space as small as a 1-foot cube and can pass through a space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The lurker can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Ward of Innocence
trait.text: The lurker can't attack or willingly move to a space within 10 feet of a creature holding a stuffed animal once owned by a child.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The lurker makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) slashing damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 14) from one of two claws. Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained.

action.name: Dark Teleport (Bonus Action)
action.text: While the lurker isn't in bright light, it teleports up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. If the destination space isn't in bright light, the lurker doesn't need to see it. A creature the lurker is grappling must succeed on a DC 13 Charisma saving throw or teleport with the lurker to an unoccupied space closest to the lurker's destination.

monster.description:
Thing That Goes Bump in the Night
Lurkers in the dark are wicked Fey creatures who prey upon the fearful, vulnerable, and alone. They resemble nightmarishly distorted people with gray skin, lank hair, and three-fingered, clawed hands. Their limbs move in impossible ways, allowing them to slip through and hide within spaces that seem far too small. They delight in stoking their victim's fears by whispering from spaces a person couldn't ft. When the terror crests, the lurkers strike, drag their victims into the dark, and vanish with them.
Secret. Frightened children know that lurkers in the dark can be held at bay by clutching a beloved stuffed animal.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 515 (Third Party)

monster.name: Maidenwood

trait.name: Undoing Gift (1/Day)
trait.text: The maidenwood must accept and eat an apple offered to it or to its lure. The maidenwood then takes 55 (10d10) poison damage and is poisoned for 1 minute.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The maidenwood makes two Tendril attacks. It can replace one attack with a Chew attack.

action.name: Tendril
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 16) from one of two tendrils. Until the grapple ends, it is restrained.

action.name: Chew
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d10 + 6) piercing damage. If the target is restrained, it takes an extra 18 (4d8) piercing damage.

action.name: Lure (Bonus Action)
action.text: The maidenwood shapes its lure into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or it retracts the lure. The maidenwood can speak through the lure and move it anywhere within reach of its tendrils. It then attempts to attract one creature within 30 feet of the lure. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw or be charmed until the start of the maidenwood's next turn. The charmed target must approach the lure as closely as it can. The maidenwood has advantage on attack rolls against the charmed target. If the target takes damage, the charmed condition ends at the end of the current turn.

reaction.name: Absorb Magic
reaction.text: If the maidenwood succeeds on a saving throw against a spell or other magical effect, or if a spell attack roll misses it, the maidenwood gains 10 temporary hit points.

monster.description:
Witch-Warped Ambushing Tree
Maidenwoods are creatures birthed from tree stumps by old magics, often arising from curses that turn the forest against a transgressor. They scuttle on roots like legs, and the broken trunk becomes a fierce, grinding maw. The maidenwood's tendrils blossom into lifelike figures it uses to lure its prey close.
Secret. The curse that maintains a maidenwood unravels if one is offered an apple-an ancient trick used by witches turned against them.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 516 (Third Party)

monster.name: Mutant Nurse

trait.name: Anchoring Mask
trait.text: The nurse has a porcelain mask (AC 15; HP 15; immunity to poison and psychic damage). If the nurse starts its turn and isn't wearing its mask, it must move as far as possible to retrieve its mask if possible, or to reach the closest creature it can see or hear, and it must attack that creature. If it can't see or hear any other creatures, it either stands still and does nothing, or it destroys any objects in its vicinity (50 percent chance of either).

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The nurse makes two Stab attacks. If it hits a creature with a Stab attack, it can use Inject if available.

action.name: Stab
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) poison damage.

action.name: Inject (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: A creature the nurse hit with a Stab attack this turn must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage and is poisoned for 1 hour. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the target also falls unconscious while poisoned in this way, until it takes damage, or until a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to shake it awake. On a successful save the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Mutation
action.text:
The nurse has one of the following mutation action options:
Cerebral Graft. Part of a psionic creature's brain is grafted to the nurse's own, and emits psychic pain. One creature within 30 feet of the nurse must make a DC 13 Intelligence saving throw, taking 11 (2d10) psychic damage and is stunned until the end of its next turn on a failure, or half as much damage on a success.
Tentacle Lash. A tentacle writhes beneath the nurse's skin or clothes, and can lash out in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage and the target is knocked prone on a failure, or half as much damage only on a success.

action.name: Catalyst (Bonus Action)
action.text: The nurse injects itself with a stimulant. Until the start of the nurse's next turn, its speed is doubled, it can make one extra Stab attack when it takes the Multiattack action, and it gains a +2 bonus to AC.

monster.description:
Caregiver Twisted by Mutation
Mutant nurses resemble the Humanoids they once were, though an alien mutation writhes within their bodies. They serve as caretakers of healing institutions, though they are fully beholden to whoever, or whatever, altered them to their current state. The nurses all wear a white porcelain mask made in the likeness of their original features, which grounds them to their sense of self and prevents them from losing their will fully to the aberrant influence that changed them.
Secret. Mutant nurses go berserk if their masks are destroyed or taken from them, or they become unresponsive if there are no other creatures nearby.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 517 (Third Party)

monster.name: Necrolisk

trait.name: Entrapping Saliva
trait.text: The necrolisk ignores immunity to the grappled and restrained conditions of a creature with the Incorporeal Movement trait. Creatures swallowed by the necrolisk can't pass through it using Incorporeal Movement or through the Ethereal Plane, and creatures can't teleport out of its stomach.

trait.name: Herb Allergy
trait.text: If the necrolisk eats a specific concoction of herbs worth 25 gp (such as if a swallowed creature pours them into its stomach as an action), at the end of the turn, the necrolisk regurgitates anything in its stomach as if it failed the saving throw described in its Swallow bonus action.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The necrolisk can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Tunneler
trait.text: The necrolisk can burrow through solid rock at half its burrowing speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The necrolisk makes one Bite attack and one Claws attack.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) force damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 16). Until the grapple ends, it is restrained.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d6 + 6) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) force damage.

action.name: Ephemeral Spit (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The necrolisk sprays spectral saliva in a 60-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line. Each creature in the area must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 42 (12d6) force damage and is restrained until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Swallow (Bonus Action)
action.text: The necrolisk makes one Bite attack against a Large or smaller creature it is grappling. If the attack hits, the target is swallowed, and the grapple ends. The swallowed target is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the necrolisk, and it takes 21 (6d6) force damage at the start of each of the necrolisk's turns. The necrolisk can only have one creature swallowed at a time. If the necrolisk takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from the swallowed creature, the necrolisk must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate the creature, which falls prone in a space within 10 feet of the necrolisk. If the necrolisk dies, a swallowed creature is no longer restrained by it and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting prone.

monster.description:
Subterranean Spirit-Devouring Drake
Necrolisks are subterranean dragons that feed on the essence of spirits. Tunneling through the ground in search of corpses, especially those buried in graveyards and crypts, they defile the graves and feed on the bodies in an effort to stir up restless spirits, which are then snared in the necrolisks' ephemeral saliva and devoured. Creatures of flesh and blood that disturb the dragons might also find themselves as a less preferred, but passable, meal.
Secret. Necrolisks are severely allergic to a concoction of lavender, nightshade, and lilies, which causes them to vomit uncontrollably-a boon to anyone swallowed by the monsters.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 518 (Third Party)

monster.name: Night Creature
monster.vulnerable: damage from weapons wiped with salt
monster.conditionImmune: charmed (except from its creator), exhaustion, poisoned

trait.name: Blood Stalker
trait.text: The night creature has advantage on Wisdom checks made to find or track a creature that is missing any hit points.

trait.name: Salt Susceptibility
trait.text: The night creature has vulnerability to damage from weapons wiped with salt. A creature can take a bonus action to wipe salt onto a weapon, which remains coated for 1 minute, or until it hits a creature.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The night creature can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
trait.text: The night creature takes 20 radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The night creature makes one Claw attack and uses Bite.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 14) from one of two claws.

action.name: Bite
action.text: One creature within 5 feet of the night creature that is willing or that's grappled, incapacitated, or restrained must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw, or it takes 6 (1d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken until it finishes a long rest, and the night creature regains hit points equal to that amount.

action.name: Shape-Shift (Bonus Action)
action.text: If the night creature isn't in sunlight, it shape-shifts into a Medium Humanoid or returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monster.description:
Bestial Blood Hunter
Night creatures are fiendish spirits bound into vampire-blood-tainted corpses through profane rituals. The hulking brutes are fanatically loyal to their creators, a fact some vampire overlords learn too late when those they employ to create the night creatures develop ambitions of their own.
Secrets. Night creatures are quickly undone by the purity of salt if wiped on a weapon and driven into a wound.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 519 (Third Party)

monster.name: Old Ways Chosen

trait.name: Animal Mask
trait.text: The chosen wears the mask of an animal, imbuing it with the strength of the old gods. When a creature is grappling the chosen, the creature can remove the mask as an action, or as an attack during the Attack action, with a successful DC 12 Strength (Athletics) check.
