Without its mask, the chosen can't cast spells, its concentration on any existing spells ends, and the save DC of Song of the Old Ways is reduced by 4.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The chosen makes three Ritual Dagger attacks.

action.name: Ritual Dagger
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d4 + 4) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) radiant or necrotic damage (the chosen's choice).

action.name: Songs of the Old Ways (Recharge 5-6)
action.text:
The chosen sings one of the following songs:Blessing of the Harvest. Each creature of the chosen's choice within 30 feet of the chosen regains 14 (4d4 + 4) hit points.
Song of Sacrifice. One creature of the chosen's choice within 30 feet must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 40 (9d8) necrotic damage and is paralyzed until the end of the chosen's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
Wicker Chant. Each creature of the chosen's choice within 20 feet of the chosen must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking 31 (9d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Spellcasting (Requires Animal Mask)
action.text:
The chosen casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components other than its Animal Mask and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks)
• At Will: Druidcraft, Minor Illusion • 2/day Each: Entangle, Scorching Ray (as 4th-level spell)

action.name: Jump (Requires Animal Mask) (Bonus Action)
action.text: The chosen casts Jump, using the same spell components and spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

monster.spells: Druidcraft, Minor Illusion, Entangle, Scorching Ray
monster.description:
Devout Follower of Old Gods
Venerating old and forgotten gods who once held sway over the remote places of the world, the Old Ways chosen keep the last embers of belief and tradition burning. Many of the chosen are insular, keeping to the communities they foster. Some are more open to contact with outsiders, whether to be a beneficent presence or to draw in potential sacrifices to the Old Ways.
Secret. Old Ways chosen bind their power into a mask they use as a focus. Without the mask, their magic unravels.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 520 (Third Party)

monster.name: Phantom hearse
monster.resist: acid, cold, fire, lightning, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks.
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained

trait.name: Breathless Warding
trait.text: A creature holding its breath is invisible to the hearse. If the creature takes damage while holding its breath, it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or let out its breath and become visible to the hearse until the start of the creature's next turn, when it can hold its breath again.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The hearse can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The hearse makes two Ghostly Coachman attacks. It can replace one of the attacks with a use of Trample, if available.

action.name: Ghostly Coachman
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) cold damage. If the target is a creature, it can't regain hit points until the start of the hearse's next turn.

action.name: Trample (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The hearse moves in a straight line up to its speed. Each creature whose space the hearse enters during this action must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 10 (3d6) cold damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 14), and it is restrained until the grapple ends. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Tolling Bell (1/Day)
action.text: The hearse rings its bell, signaling the doom of its foes. Each creature of the hearse's choice that it can see within 30 feet of the hearse must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 44 (8d10) psychic damage and its speed is halved until the start of the hearse's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Entomb (Bonus Action)
action.text: One Medium or smaller creature grappled by the hearse must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or be entombed in a coffin in the hearse, and the grapple ends. While entombed, the creature is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the hearse, and at the start of each of the hearse's turns it takes 21 (6d6) necrotic damage and gains 1 level of exhaustion. The hearse can have up to three creatures grappled at a time. If the hearse takes 20 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside a coffin, the hearse must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or release all entombed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 5 feet of the hearse and is knocked prone. If the hearse dies, any entombed creature falls to the ground prone in the hearse's former space.

monster.description:
Ghostly Coach of the Dead
Phantom hearses are ghostly carriages complete with horses and coachmen—amalgamations of tormented souls driven to claim the living and drag them to the land of the dead. The spirit coachmen strike with phantom whips and weapons, and even fly out to harry foes at a distance, while the ghostly horses drive the hearse in a crushing charge that gathers victims and seals them into a spectral coffin that drains the warmth and vitality from flesh and bone. The tolling bell on the carriage precedes the apparition's appearance, and its sepulchral sound has caused more than a few witnesses to drop dead on the spot.
Secret. Phantom hearses are driven to collect the living, and they can't see a living creature that holds its breath.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 521 (Third Party)

monster.name: Pigeon Hag
monster.skill: Deception +11, Insight +9, Perception +9, Sleight of Hand +7

trait.name: Coven Magic
trait.text: While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability: Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Lucky Charm* (lasts until the hag casts it again, or chooses to end it with no action required), or Unseen Servant. The hag must finish a long rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

trait.name: Disarming Vanity
trait.text: As an action, a creature that flatters the hag forces it to succeed on a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or be incapacitated until the end of the hag's next turn.

trait.name: Harrying Wings
trait.text: When the hag rolls initiative, it is surrounded by fey spirits in the form of angry pigeons. The hag's enemies within 10 feet of it have disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls. This trait doesn't function if the hag is incapacitated.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The hag makes three Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting (at will spells only)

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) psychic damage.

action.name: Agonizing Cackle (1/Day)
action.text: The hag laughs maniacally, and each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a DC 19 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 45 (10d8) psychic damage and is incapacitated until the start of the hag's next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 19):
• At Will: Command (as 2nd-level spell), Hold Person, Minor Illusion, Stinking Cloud • 1/day Each: Feeblemind, Dominate Person, Scrying, Word of Recall (lair as sanctuary)

action.name: Shape-Shif (Bonus Action)
action.text: The hag shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or it returns to its true form. Other than its size, its game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monster.spells: Command, Hold Person, Minor Illusion, Stinking Cloud, Feeblemind, Dominate Person, Scrying, Word of Recall
monster.description:
Vermin Hag of Deception and Control
Pigeon hags are vain witches who place themselves above every other being. They seek to reinforce that belief by controlling and undermining the knowledge and relationships of others. Pigeon hags often insinuate themselves into a mortal settlement and walk among the people, usually in a position to hear and spread gossip that they can twist and manipulate to their own ends.
Secret. The vanity of a pigeon hag is so overwhelming that polishing its ego can leave it flustered and briefly unable to act.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 522 (Third Party)

monster.name: Pumpkin Head
monster.languages: understands Common plus one other language, but only speaks using Mimicry

trait.name: Mimicry
trait.text: The pumpkin head can mimic the voice of the person it replaced or someone it used Personality Consumption on. A creature that hears the voice can tell it's an imitation only with a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Insight) check.

trait.name: Sorrowful Memento
trait.text: If the pumpkin head can see a creature holding a possession of the person the pumpkin head replaced, it has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The pumpkin head makes one Claw attack and one Pitchfork attack, and it can use Personality Consumption if available.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Pitchfork
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the pumpkin head can push or pull it 5 feet.

action.name: Personality Consumption (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The pumpkin head consumes the memories of one creature the pumpkin head can see within 30 feet of itself. The target must succeed on a DC 12 Intelligence saving throw or it becomes stunned until the end of its next turn, and its Intelligence score decreases by 1d4. The target dies if this reduces that score to 0. A Humanoid slain in this way is replaced by a pumpkin head 1d8 hours later unless its body is burned. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest.

monster.description:
Fiendish Effigy of the Fields
Pumpkin heads are scarecrows of straw, sticks, and clothing topped with a carved pumpkin for a head and animated by a fiendish spirit. They are crafted by demons to resemble their Humanoid victims and returned to wherever the unfortunate souls had been snatched from. These living effigies speak with the voice of whomever they replaced, often repeating phrases and behaviors that the missing person said or did in life while stalking and killing new prey.
Secret. Pumpkin heads hold a shred of memory from the people they replaced. The sight of a keepsake or memento from a pumpkin head's victim confuses the creature.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 523 (Third Party)

monster.name: Reveler of Song

trait.name: Compelled Contest
trait.text: The reveler can't refuse a contest of skill with musical instruments. As an action, a creature can make a Charisma (Performance) check using a musical instrument. The reveler must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to the check's total or take 20 psychic damage and be stunned until the end of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Dancing Step
trait.text: The reveler doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it leaves an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Ensemble Form
trait.text: The reveler takes on a specific form: Drone, Drum, or Bell. The form determines certain traits and actions, as well as the instrument the reveler plays (hurdy gurdy, frame drum, or handbells, respectively).

trait.name: Droning Swarm (Drone Only)
trait.text: A droning swarm of bees surrounds the reveler. Any creature that ends its turn within 10 feet of the reveler must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The reveler makes two Instrumental Blast attacks.

action.name: Instrumental Blast
action.text: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 90 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) thunder damage.

action.name: Infectious Rhythm (Drum Only)
action.text: The reveler plays a thrilling beat. Up to two creatures of the reveler's choice that it can see within 30 feet must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 18 (4d8) psychic damage and is affected by the Irresistible Dance spell (no concentration required to maintain the spell). On a failed save, a target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Stunning Clangor (Bell Only)
action.text: The reveler rings its bell. One creature of the reveler's choice that the reveler can see within 60 feet of itself must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 40 (9d8) thunder damage and is stunned until the end of the reveler's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

monster.description:
Fiend of Music and Dance
Revelers of song are Fiends of music and abandon who turn their art against their foes in destructive reverberation. The revelers dance and play at nearly all times and are known to fy into a murderous rage if their invitations to join the celebration are spurned. A variety of revelers of song exist, depending on the type of instrument a given reveler plays. Drone revelers play the hurdy gurdy and are surrounded by an accompanying swarm of bees. Drum revelers play drums or cymbals, and the world shakes to their beat. Bell revelers play some variety of bell, chime, or gong, and the ringing overtakes all other sounds.
Secret. Revelers of song can't accept that any creature is more skilled at an instrument than they are and so can't refuse a musical challenge.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 524 (Third Party)

monster.name: Reveler of Stories

trait.name: Moral Quandary
trait.text: As a bonus action, a creature can recite the moral of a story using the same theme as the reveler's Storied Form to weaken it for 1 hour. While weakened, the reveler can't take reactions, and its AC is reduced by 4.

trait.name: Storied Form
trait.text: The reveler takes on a specific form: Great Beast, Hero, or Witch. The form determines certain traits and the theme of stories the reveler embodies (powerful monsters, brave heroes, or wicked magicians, respectively).

trait.name: Terror Feeding (Witch Only)
trait.text: If there is at least one frightened creature within 30 feet of the reveler at the end of its turn, the reveler gains 15 temporary hit points.

trait.name: Additional Armor Class (Hero Only)
trait.text: The Hero gains +2 to their AC due to their shield.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The reveler makes three Thrash attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Fabled Assault if available.

action.name: Thrash
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) force damage.

action.name: Fabled Assault (Recharge 5-6)
action.text:
The reveler uses the option determined by its Storied Form:
Consuming Bite (Great Beast Only). One creature within 5 feet must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw (with disadvantage if the reveler is grappling the target). On a failed save, a the target takes 36 (8d8) necrotic damage, and if it's Huge or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 15) and restrained until the grapple ends. On a successful saving throw the target takes half as much damage only. The reveler gains temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage taken.
Heroic Charge (Hero Only). The reveler moves up to its speed in a straight line without provoking opportunity attacks, and it can move through other creatures without expending extra movement to do so. Each creature whose space the reveler enters must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 26 (4d12) force damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.
Wicked Storm (Witch Only). The reveler summons a crackling storm in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 27 (6d8) lightning damage and becomes frightened for 1 minute. While frightened in this way, the target's speed is 0. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only. A creature frightened by the storm repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Dramatic Reversal
reaction.text: When the reveler is hit by an attack roll, the reveler adds 4 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

monster.description:
Fiend of Legend and Folklore
Timeless stories have inspired countless mortals, and the echoes of those stories in the Lower Planes have spawned fiendish embodiments of those tales—the revelers of stories. These Fiends twist the themes of their tales, turning inspiration into doom. The revelers' forms and powers change depending on the theme of stories they embody. Great beast revelers of stories rise from tales of terrible monsters. Hero revelers of stories embody the exploits of knights and other mighty heroes. Witch revelers of stories bring to life tales of wicked witches, wizards, and other magicians.
Secret. Revelers of stories exist because of fables, legends, and other tales, and reciting the moral of a story related to the reveler's theme robs the reveler of its potency.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 526 (Third Party)

monster.name: Rotweaver

trait.name: Fungal Step
trait.text: The rotweaver ignores difficult terrain caused by plants or fungi.

trait.name: Hunting Horn
trait.text: The rotweaver carries a hunting horn. The horn has AC 16, HP 10, and immunity to necrotic, poison, and psychic damage.

trait.name: Iron Binding
trait.text: If the rotweaver has iron wrapped around a part of its body, such as an iron bracelet, manacles, or chain (treat any of them as applying manacles), the rotweaver can only do one of the following on its turn: move, take an action, or take a bonus action.

action.name: Staff
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) necrotic damage.

action.name: Call the Rot (Recharge 5-6) (Requires Hunting Horn)
action.text: The rotweaver blows its horn, unleashing rot in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 11 (2d10) necrotic damage and is poisoned until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a targettakes half as much damage only. The horn call is audible out to 300 feet, and any non-creature plants in the cone die from fungus and rot.

monster.description:
Fey Thrall of Rot
Rotweavers were Fey caretakers of the natural world or fey planes who've been corrupted by the power of rot. Rotweavers serve powerful beings of withering and blight as minor acolytes and hunters, working to spread the bounds of their beloved decay.
Secret. The rotweavers' fey nature still holds; binding them with iron, such as manacles or a chain, mutes their strength.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 528 (Third Party)

monster.name: Serum Brute
monster.senses: blindsight 60 ft. (blind beyond this radius unless Unleashed Eyes is active)

trait.name: Curse Dependent
trait.text: If the brute is targeted by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic, until it finishes a long rest, the brute loses its blindsight, its Withering Eye trait, and its Cursed Gaze action.

trait.name: Stimulant Rush
trait.text:
At the start of its turn, the brute must choose one of the following options (it can't use that option twice in a row):
Catalyst Infusion. Until the start of its next turn, the brute's speed increases to 50 feet, it doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it leaves an enemy's reach, and its long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet with or without a running start.
Caustic Secretion. Until the start of its next turn, its Fist attack deals an extra 11 (2d10) acid damage (included in the Fist attack below).
Stim Boost. Gain 10 temporary hit points.

trait.name: Unleashed Eyes (1/Day)
trait.text: When the brute has half its hit points or fewer, its sealed eyes rip open and unleash its curse. It is no longer blind beyond 60 feet, and it can use its Withering Eye trait and Cursed Gaze action until it finishes a long rest, whereupon its eyes seal shut again.

trait.name: Withering Eye (Unleashed Eyes Only)
trait.text: When the brute moves to within 5 feet of a creature, the creature takes 4 (1d8) psychic damage. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The brute makes two Fist attacks and can use Cursed Gaze if available.

action.name: Fist
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage. If Caustic Secretion is active, the target takes an extra 11 (2d10) acid damage.

action.name: Cursed Gaze (Unleashed Eyes Only)
action.text: The brute glares with its opened third eye. Each creature in a 30-foot cone must make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw, taking 9 (2d8) psychic damage and becoming frightened until the start of the brute's next turn on a failed save, or it takes half as much damage only on a successful one.

reaction.name: Devotion
reaction.text: When one of the brute's allies within 5 feet of it takes damage, the ally gains resistance to the triggering damage, and the brute takes an amount of psychic damage equal to the damage the ally takes.

monster.description:
Cursed Giant Enhanced by Alchemy
Serum brutes are descended from giants who suffered an enduring curse when they failed to take over a Fey domain. They have third eyes, usually in their foreheads, which channel the giants' curse into the world around them. All three of their eyes are sewn or otherwise sealed shut to contain the curse. Serum brutes are further enhanced with—and get their name from—a series of alchemical infusions, often delivered through implanted vessels and tubes, which seek to deaden their sorrow and pain, as well as the disturbing power of their third eyes. They form deep emotional bonds with any who offer them comfort and an escape from the pain of their curse, and they are often willing to die for those allies.
Secret. Serum brutes are dependent upon their curse, and magic that breaks curses can leave the giants debilitated and bereft.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 529 (Third Party)

monster.name: Shrouded
monster.resist: acid, cold, fire, lightning, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, prone, restrained

trait.name: Mist Form
trait.text: The shrouded can enter another creature's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing.

trait.name: Corrupting Mist
trait.text: A creature that starts its turn in the shrouded's space must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The soul of a Humanoid that dies from this damage becomes a new shrouded at the start of the shrouded's next turn and takes its turn on that initiative. The Humanoid can't be returned to life until the new shrouded is destroyed.

trait.name: Graveyard Warding
trait.text: The shrouded can't move through a space with a barrier or line of iron, such as a wrought iron fence or a line of iron flings. This impassible barrier rises to a height of 1 mile above the iron.

trait.name: Variation
trait.text: The shrouded has one of three variants, which determines a unique action option: Devourer, Reaper, or Whisperer.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The shrouded makes two melee attacks.

action.name: Corrupting Touch
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) necrotic damage.

action.name: Consuming Bite (Devourer Only)
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 13). At the start of a grappled target's turn, it takes 10 (3d6) necrotic damage, and the shrouded regains hit points equal to that amount.

action.name: Scythe (Reaper Only)
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 10 (3d6) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it can't regain hit points until the start of the shrouded's next turn.

action.name: Toxic Thoughts (Whisperer Only)
action.text: The shrouded fills the area within 30 feet of it with mental anguish. Each creature that isn't a Construct or Undead within that area must make a DC 13 Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 7 (2d6) psychic damage and is frightened until the start of the shrouded's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

monster.description:
Soul Corrupted by the Shroud
Shrouded are the spirits of the dead—devoid of any ability to reason and corrupted by the deathly mists known as the Shroud. They are raw emotional energy, unfettered and unleashed, manifested into being. The shrouded are constantly whispering about a victim's incompetencies, weakness, foibles, and failings as they engulf their prey to drain the life from them, corrupting their soul.
Secret. Shrouded are bound by barriers and lines of iron, which is why cemeteries with iron fences keep them contained.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 530 (Third Party)

monster.name: Skitterswarm
monster.languages: understands Common and Sylvan but can't spea k
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, prone, restrained, stunned

trait.name: Body Burrowing
trait.text: If the swarm can see the corpse of a Humanoid at the start of its turn, it must move up to its speed toward the corpse. Once it enters the corpse's space, it must remain there for 1 hour, after which time the corpse's flesh putrefies.

trait.name: Swarm
trait.text: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny centipede. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Vile Vermin
trait.text: Any creature that starts its turn in the swarm's space must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or contract a wasting affliction. While afflicted, the target can't regain hit points, and it repeats the save when it finishes a long rest or when targeted by an effect that ends the poisoned condition, recovering from the affliction on a success. A creature that succeeds on the initial saving throw is immune to the Vile Vermin trait of all skitterswarms for 24 hours.

action.name: Infesting Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d6) piercing damage, or 3 (1d6) piercing damage if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer. If the target is a creature, a centipede infests its body. While infested, the target takes 3 (1d6) necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. The infestation ends when the target regains hit points or is targeted by an effect that ends the poisoned condition.

monster.description:
Fetid Fey Centipedes
Disgusting Fey who delight in warping flesh and spreading sickness can gather into squirming masses of centipedes known as skitterswarms. These swarms lurk in places where foulness accumulates and often serve centipede hags.
Secret. Skitterswarms can't resist dead bodies and are drawn to the corpses of Humanoids.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 531 (Third Party)

monster.name: Soulfire Stoker
monster.conditionImmune: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious

trait.name: Corpse Fuel
trait.text: If the stoker can see a corpse at the start of its turn, it must move as far as possible to enter the corpse's space and use its action to incinerate the corpse. Medium or smaller corpses are reduced to ash, and larger corpses are partially incinerated and decrease one size.

trait.name: Fire Form
trait.text: The stoker can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing, and it can enter a creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes 4 (1d8) fire damage.

trait.name: Illumination
trait.text: The stoker sheds dim light in a 30-foot radius.

trait.name: Soul Burn
trait.text: At the end of each of the stoker's turns, each creature within 5 feet of the stoker takes 4 (1d8) necrotic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The stoker makes two Sear attacks.

action.name: Sear
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) fire damage, and the target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the start of the elemental's next turn.

action.name: Spectral Steam (Recharge 5-6) (Bonus Action)
action.text: The stoker unleashes a blast of glowing spectral steam in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius for 1 minute, and for the duration, it can't hide or benefit from being invisible.

monster.description:
Engine-Born Spirit of Flame and Death
Soulfire stokers are Elemental spirits of fire who inhabit furnaces, engines, and boilers that burn bodies or souls. The resulting creature is composed of ghostly flames and steam, with swirling plates of phantom iron that resemble the engine or machine that gave it form. A crackling blend of fire and negative energy, soulfire stokers burn away souls as much as flesh.
Secret. Soulfire stokers are compelled to burn corpses they can see, so dead things can bait the spirits.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 532 (Third Party)

monster.name: Squabswarm
monster.languages: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, prone, restrained, stunned

trait.name: Compelled Delivery
trait.text: As an action, a creature can direct the swarm to deliver a letter or parcel (which the creature must provide) to a recipient the creature knows. The swarm is charmed until it delivers the item to the specified recipient or until it takes damage. While charmed in this way, the swarm must deliver the item to the specified recipient if it knows where the recipient is.

trait.name: Swarm
trait.text: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny pigeon. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Unerring Delivery
trait.text: If the swarm is tasked to deliver an item to a creature, the swarm knows the distance and direction to the creature while they are on the same plane.

action.name: Peck
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (2d4) piercing damage, or 2 (1d4) piercing damage if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer. If the swarm has over half its hit points and the target is a creature, the target is charmed until the start of the swarm's next turn.

reaction.name: Many Eyes, One Voice (1/Day)
reaction.text: When a creature who tasked the swarm to deliver an item and who is on the same plane as the swarm takes an action to see through the swarm, the swarm becomes a scrying sensor as if the creature cast Scrying. For the spell's duration, the creature can speak through the swarm.

monster.description:
Peeping Fey Pigeons
Fey messengers that take the form of pigeons can gather into flocks, or squabswarms. Hags and other influential Fey use squabswarms as spies and messengers—and sometimes as unerring trackers to locate any who fee responsibility or judgement.
Secret. Squabswarms' drive to deliver letters or parcels is overwhelming, and they can be confused by delivery requests.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 533 (Third Party)

monster.name: Vermin Familiar
monster.senses: blindsight 30 ft. (Pigeon only), darkvision 60 ft., tremorsense 30 ft. (Centipede only)

trait.name: Disparate Incarnation
trait.text: The familiar incarnates in a body similar to a pest: Centipede, Pigeon, or Weasel, which determines other traits and actions the familiar has.

trait.name: Fear of Canines
trait.text: If the familiar can see a dog, wolf, or other canine at the start of its turn, it becomes frightened until the start of its next turn. While frightened in this way, it has disadvantage on saving throws.

trait.name: Flyby (Pigeon Only)
trait.text: The familiar doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Spider Climb (Centipede Only)
trait.text: The familiar can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Vermin Telepathy
trait.text: The familiar can magically control Beasts and swarms that match its incarnation (centipedes, pigeons, weasels, giant versions of each, as well as Boogleswarms, Skitterswarms, and Squabswarms) within 120 feet of itself, using a special telepathy.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The familiar makes two Rend attacks.

action.name: Rend
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) psychic damage.

action.name: Incarnated Assault (Recharge 5-6)
action.text:
The familiar uses the option that matches its incarnation.
Confounding Scratch (Weasel Only). One creature the familiar can see within 5 feet of it must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 21 (6d6) necrotic damage and is affected by the Confusion spell until the start of the familiar's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
Infectious Sting (Centipede Only). One creature the familiar can see within 5 feet of it must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 10 (3d6) poison damage and is poisoned until the end of the familiar's next turn. While poisoned in this way, the target is paralyzed. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
Terrifying Flutter (Pigeon Only). One creature the familiar can see within 5 feet of it must make a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 21 (6d6) psychic damage and is frightened until the end of the familiar's next turn. The frightened target uses its movement on its turn to get as far away as possible from the familiar, moving by the safest route. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Invisibility
action.text: The familiar casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability.

monster.description:
Infesting Hag Companion
Capricious Fey spirits incarnated into bodies reminiscent of normal pests, vermin familiars serve spellcasters and especially vermin hags. While they are most often drawn to serve wicked masters, they also delight in playing the part of dutiful servants to corrupt spellcasters who aren't evil.
Secret. Vermin familiars are gripped by deep-seated fears shared by the vermin forms they inhabit, and as such, are terrified of dogs and other canines.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 534 (Third Party)

monster.name: Vermin Infestation
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, prone, restrained, stunned
monster.senses: blindsight 30 ft., darkvision 60 ft., tremorsense 30 ft.

trait.name: Infghting
trait.text: As an action, a creature that insinuates that the components of the swarm are conspiring against each other and succeeds on a DC 15 Charisma (Deception) check deals 10 necrotic damage to the swarm.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The swarm can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Swarm
trait.text: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can fit through any opening large enough for a Tiny creature. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

action.name: Shred
action.text:
Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (4d4 + 4) piercing damage, or 9 (2d4 + 4) piercing damage if the swarm has half its hit points or fewer. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature in the swarm's space, the target is grappled (escape DC 14). The swarm can have one creature grappled at a time. At the start of each of the grappled target's turns, roll 1d6 to see which of the following effects it suffers:
1–2: Centipede Sting. The target takes 4 (1d8) poison damage and is poisoned until the start of its next turn.
3–4: Pigeon Cry. The target takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage.
5–6: Weasel Drain. The target takes 5 (1d10) necrotic damage, and its hit point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken until it finishes a long rest.

monster.description:
Swarm of Verminous Familiars
A nightmarish amalgamation of centipedes, pigeons, and weasels, vermin infestations are thankfully rare. These swarms of Fey roil and scratch and rend with individual pigeons streaking out to tear at distant foes, becoming a firenzied mass that throws all subtlety and coordination to the wind. At times, the constituent vermin-like Fey seem to be at each other's throats as much as any victims of the swarm.
Secret. The Fey spirits that make up a vermin infestation loathe one another and can be tricked into fighting among themselves by a clever suggestion.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 535 (Third Party)

monster.name: Wayward Haint
monster.languages: acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, piercing, slashing, thunder
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained

trait.name: Bottle Trap
trait.text: A haint that enters the space of an upright, empty bottle must succeed on a DC 16 Charisma saving throw or be drawn into the bottle, and a cork or stopper appears to close the bottle. The trapped haint can see and hear through the bottle out to 10 feet, and any creature in that range can hear the haint. The haint is released if the bottle breaks or another creature opens it.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The haint can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside a solid object.

action.name: Draining Regret
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 10 (3d6) psychic damage, and the target gains 1 level of exhaustion. This can't raise the target's exhaustion level above 5, and all levels of exhaustion gained from this action end when the target finishes a short or long rest, or if it receives a Lesser Restoration spell or similar magic.

monster.description:
Lost Soul Bound to Regret
Wayward haints are restless spirits stuck between the realms of the living and the dead. Be it a curse or an unresolved confict from their previous life, a haint can't rest until their unfinished business is resolved. Haints only show hints of who they were in life, taking on a more symbolic form related to the hurt or loss they can't let go of. They tend to repeat their monotonous actions—often imitating those performed in life or reenacting their sorrow—over and over for eternity. When provoked, they can display a volatile temper and lash out with a crushing wave of the grief and indecision that binds their spirit.
Secret. Wayward haints can be trapped in empty bottles if they're tricked or forced to pass over one.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 536 (Third Party)

monster.name: Weasel Hag

trait.name: Biting Gift
trait.text: If a creature willingly offers one of its teeth to the hag, the hag must accept it and be charmed for 24 hours, or until the creature or its allies deal damage to the hag or force it to make a saving throw.

trait.name: Coven Magic
trait.text: While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability: Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Lucky Charm (lasts until the hag casts it again, or chooses to end it with no action required), or Unseen Servant. The hag must finish a long rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The hag has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The hag makes one Bite attack and uses Spellcasting (at will spells only).

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) psychic damage.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13).
• At Will: Blindness/Deafness, Detect Magic, Ray of Enfeeblement • 1/day Each: Confusion, Dream

action.name: Waking Terrors (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The hag assaults the mind of one creature it can see within 30 feet of itself. The target must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw (with disadvantage If the hag has one of the target's teeth). On a failed save, the target takes 21 (6d6) psychic damage and is frightened for 1 minute. While frightened in this way, the target perceives its allies as enemies born from its nightmares, but it isn't compelled to act against or harm them. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. At the end of each of its turns, a frightened target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Shape-Shift (Bonus Action)
action.text: The hag shape-shifts to resemble a Tiny weasel (walking speed 30 ft., climbing speed 30 ft.) or a Small or Medium Humanoid, or back into her true form. Her game statistics, other than her speed as a weasel and her size, are the same in each form. Any equipment she is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monster.spells: Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Lucky Charm, Unseen Servant, Blindness/Deafness, Detect Magic, Ray of Enfeeblement, Confusion, Dream
monster.description:
Hag of Soul Twisting and Terror
Covetous and bedecked in stolen and gifted teeth, weasel hags are Fey creatures that sow nightmares and temptation to wickedness. They often surround themselves with weasels and boogleswarms, using them as spies, thieves to steal trinkets (especially lost teeth), and to spread their malign influence.
Secret. Weasel hags' love of teeth goes beyond a mere quirk. They can't refuse a tooth willingly given by its owner and can't harm the owner for a time.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 537 (Third Party)

monster.name: Wellwyrm

trait.name: Amphibious
trait.text: The wellwyrm can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Hunting Terror
trait.text: If the wellwyrm hears a creature play a horn or similar musical instrument, it becomes frightened until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The wellwyrm makes one Bite attack and uses Constrict.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) piercing damage plus 21 (6d6) poison damage.

action.name: Constrict
action.text: One Medium or smaller creature the wellwyrm can see within 5 feet of itself must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or take 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage and be grappled (escape DC 14). The target is restrained until the grapple ends. The wellwyrm needn't spend extra movement to move during this grapple.

monster.description:
Serpent Lurking Beneath the Surface
Wellwyrms are serpents originally touched by cruel Fey masters. The wyrms live in shallow waters where land-dwelling creatures firequent, such as wells and watering holes, lying in wait to coil around unsuspecting victims. Wellwyrms then drag their prey into water, or into burrows they dig under seemingly safe ground, to drown or suffocate.
Secret. The sound of a hunting horn drives wellwyrms into a panicked frenzy.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 538 (Third Party)

monster.name: Wildpyre
monster.resist: acid, lightning, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The wildpyre can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Quenching Water
trait.text: Any water afects the wildpyre as if it were holy water.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The wildpyre makes two Blazing Rend attacks and uses Flame Bind if available.

action.name: Blazing Rend
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) necrotic damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage. If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it ignites. Until a creature within 5 feet of the target takes an action to douse the flames, the target takes 2 (1d4) fire damage at the start of each of its turns. If the target is a creature no larger than the wildpyre, it must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Flame Bind (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The wildpyre creates a ring of ghostly flame around a creature it can see within 60 feet of itself. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or become restrained. At the end of each of the target's turns, it repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. During the target's turn, it can choose to end the restrained condition by taking 6 (1d12) fire damage plus 6 (1d12) necrotic damage.

monster.description:
Spectral Blazing Beast
When animals perish in flames, their escaping spirits can cling to the fire that took their lives and arise as wildpyres. These Undead resemble the kind of animal they were in life, though made of ghostly flames. They lash out at anything in their vicinity, fanning the flames that consumed them.
Secret. Water can extinguish the undying flames of wildpyres, despite their ephemeral existence.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 539 (Third Party)

monster.name: Woodwarped
monster.vulnerable: slashing from battleaxes, greataxes, and handaxes
monster.immune: deafened, exhaustion, frightened (except by creatures holding an axe)

trait.name: Aversion to Axes
trait.text: At the start of the woodwarped's turn, if it can see a creature holding a battleaxe, a greataxe, or a handaxe, it becomes frightened until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Thorns
trait.text: At the start of each of its turns, the woodwarped deals 5 (2d4) piercing damage to any creature it is grappling or any creature grappling it.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The woodwarped makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) poison damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 14) from one of two claws.

action.name: Thorn Spray (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The woodwarped violently expels thorns around itself. Each creature within 15 feet of the woodwarped must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw (with disadvantage if grappled by it). On a failed save, a target takes 14 (4d6) piercing damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A poisoned creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

reaction.name: Splintered Propagation (1/Day)
reaction.text: When the woodwarped takes slashing damage that isn't from a battleaxe, greataxe, or handaxe, a fragment of the woodwarped breaks of and rapidly grows into a new woodwarped in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the original. The new woodwarped is Small, has 10 hit points, only has the Claw action, lacks this reaction, and takes its turn on the original woodwarped's initiative.

monster.description:
Facsimile Grown of Root and Vine
Whispered among forest dwellers is the tale of a cursed vine, said to creep into the bodies of the living or the dead, twisting them into woodwarped. These wooden creations of tangled roots and bark take shape into a crude yet eerily familiar imitation of their hosts. Those that slay their hosts are sometimes clad in the stolen clothing and armor of their victims, silently wandering the woods with hollow eyes that reflect the unnatural force that binds them to the forest. Cutting pieces from their bodies is a dangerous proposition, as the fragments quickly grow into new woodwarped.
Secret. Woodwarped possess a deep fear of axes—tools capable of severing not only their bodies but their bond to the forest. The mere sight of an axe is enough to hold the creatures at bay, and the bite of its blade slices them like smoke.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 540 (Third Party)

monster.name: Wormhost Surgeon

trait.name: Regeneration
trait.text: The surgeon regains 10 hit points at the start of each of its turns. If it takes poison damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. The surgeon dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

trait.name: Veiled Ignorance
trait.text: A creature whose mind can't be read, or that is under the effect of a Mind Blank or Nondetection spell, becomes invisible toward the surgeon (this ignores the surgeon's blindsight).

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The surgeon makes two Bone Saw attacks.

action.name: Bone Saw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) psychic damage. On a critical hit, if the target is a creature, its Intelligence score decreases by 1d4. The target dies if this reduces that score to 0. Otherwise, the reduction lasts until the target finishes a long rest.

action.name: Infested Spray (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The surgeon unleashes a spray of worm-ridden cerebral fuid in a 20-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 17 (5d6) psychic damage, and it is infested with larval parasitic worms. An infested target takes 7 (2d6) psychic damage at the end of each of its turns as the worms burrow toward its brain. The ongoing damage ends when the target takes poison damage or is targeted by an effect that ends the poisoned condition. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. If the target is reduced to 0 hit points by this psychic damage, the worms reach its brain and it dies. A Humanoid slain in this way rises 24 hours later as a wormhost surgeon.

reaction.name: Protective Parasite
reaction.text: When the surgeon is reduced to 0 hit points, the parasite worm emerges from the surgeon's exposed brain and spits acid in a 20-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line. Each creature in that area must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

monster.description:
Parasitical Physician
Alien parasitic worms that infest the brains of Humanoid bodies mutate their hosts into wormhost surgeons. These aberrant husks are obsessed with anatomical study and with finding new Humanoids to infest with larval worm slurry to propagate their kind. They can pass for ordinary people in most cases but must take care to hide their exposed brains and the wriggling worms within their flesh.
Secret. Wormhost surgeons are drawn to seek out and infest sapient victims, so intelligent creatures with veiled minds slip beneath the surgeons' notice.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 541 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Barrow King

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the king fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The king has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Marshall Undead
trait.text: Undead creatures of the king's choice (excluding himself) within 60 feet of him have advantage on attack rolls and saving throws. He can't use this trait if he is incapacitated.

trait.name: Name of Rest
trait.text: The king has AC 10 against attack rolls made with a weapon inscribed with the runic form of his original name, and the king has disadvantage on attack rolls made against a creature holding such a weapon.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The king makes three Soul Blade attacks. He can replace one attack with a use of Life Drain.

action.name: Soul Blade
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 40/80 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d6 + 7) slashing damage plus 13 (3d8) necrotic damage. Hit or Miss: The blade magically returns to the king's hand immediately after a ranged attack.

action.name: Life Drain
action.text: The king touches a creature. The target must succeed on a DC 22 Constitution saving throw or take 16 (2d8 + 7) necrotic damage, and the target's hit point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken until it finishes a long rest. A Humanoid slain by this attack rises 24 hours later as a Wight under the king's control, unless the Humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed.

action.name: Blade Storm (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The king's blade flies in a deadly arc around him. Each creature of the king's choice within 20 feet of him must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 35 (10d6) force damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Undying Mount (Recharge 5-6) (Bonus Action)
action.text: The king casts Phantom Steed, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. The conjured steed takes no damage from areas of effect. The spell ends early if the king casts it again.

reaction.name: Parry
reaction.text: When the king is hit by an attack roll while holding a weapon or shield, he adds 7 to his AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

legendary.name: Legendary Actions (3/Turn, or 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The king can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The king regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

legendary.name: Death Grasp
legendary.text: One creature the king can see within 100 feet of him must succeed on a DC 22 Strength saving throw or a ghostly, skeletal arm pulls the target up to 40 feet closer to the king, and the target's speed is halved until the end of its next turn.

legendary.name: Grim Charge
legendary.text: The king moves up to his speed (or commands his mount to move up to its speed) and makes one Soul Blade attack.

legendary.name: Lord's Call (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The king magically summons two Wights into unoccupied spaces he can see within 30 feet of himself. The wights obey the king and take their turn immediately after him on the same initiative count. A wight vanishes after 1 hour, when it or the king dies, or when the king uses this action again. A creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of a wight must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 5 (1d10) Psychic damage and be frightened until the start of its next turn. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

legendary.name: Barrow King's Lair
legendary.text: The Barrow King's lair is his burial mound and the surrounding region of resting places for his vast army. If encountered in his lair, the Barrow King has a challenge rating of 23 (50,000 XP)

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Barrow King's lair is changed by his presence, creating the following effects:
Empire of Desecration. A Hallow spell cast within 1 mile of the lair fails, but any resources used to cast it aren't wasted.
Necrotic Shroud. When a living creature within 1 mile of the lair regains hit points as part of finishing a long rest, it can only regain up to half its hit point maximum.
If the Barrow King dies or moves his lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Undying Monarch of the Damned
The Barrow King is known only by the armies of Undead he commands, which all slumber in tombs and burial mounds. Wights and their zombie conscripts arise when trespassers violate the resting place of the king's army, and tales speak of a tide of death that will sweep across the world if the king himself should awaken. Records of the king's living exploits and identity are near nonexistent, but rumors abound, and the storm of stories and myths may conceal grains of truth.
Secret. The Barrow King's only true weakness is his name, written in the runic form of his lost native language. A weapon inscribed with his runic name becomes his bane.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 542 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Beast of Blight
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, poisone d

trait.name: Blighted Overgrowth
trait.text: While the beast has half its hit points or fewer, it has disadvantage on attack rolls, its Spew Blight action recharges at the start of its turn, and if it has at least 1 hit point at the start of its turn, it regains 20 hit points. If it takes fire damage, it doesn't regain hit points at the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Fungal Step
trait.text: The beast ignores difficult terrain caused by plants or fungi.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the beast fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Unraveled Longing
trait.text: If the beast can see the likeness of its long-lost love at the start of its turn, the beast takes 10 psychic damage and has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The beast makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d10 + 7) slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 17) from one of two claws. If the beast moved at least 20 feet immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) poison damage.

action.name: Project Image
action.text: The beast casts the Project Image spell, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability. During the spell's duration, the beast can use its Spew Blight action and Rot Stride bonus action as if it were in the image's space.

action.name: Spew Blight (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The beast expels rot in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 18 (4d8) necrotic damage and is poisoned for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A poisoned creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Rot Stride (Bonus Action)
action.text: The beast teleports to an unoccupied space within 60 feet. Either its starting or destination space must contain fungus or rotting organic matter.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The beast can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The beast regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Trample
legendary.text: The beast moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks. A Large or smaller creature in a space the beast enters must succeed on a DC 19 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

legendary.name: Impale (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: A creature the beast is grappling must make a DC 19 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 14 (4d6) piercing damage and is impaled on one of the beast's exposed ribs, transferred from the claw. The target is restrained until the grapple ends. The beast can have two creatures impaled at a time. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

legendary.name: Hallucinate (Costs 3 Actions)
legendary.text: The beast exhales hallucinatory spores that fll a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on a point within 60 feet. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or be affected by the Confusion spell until the start of the beast's next turn.

legendary.name: The Beast of Blight's Lair
legendary.text: The Beast of Blight's lair is a cave in the heart of a rotten forest where blight creeps endlessly forth.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Beast of Blight's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Fouling Blight. Within 6 miles of the lair, ability checks made to forage for food or water have disadvantage, and magic that creates food or water only produces half the normal amount.
Rapid Decay. Within 1 mile of the lair, objects that aren't worn or carried have vulnerability to necrotic damage, and the length of time in which a dead creature can be brought back to life is halved.
If the Beast of Blight dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Manifestation of Rot and Decay
The Beast of Blight was once known as Farryn of the Greenwood, a druidic protector of the natural cycle who delved into forbidden lore to save her dead lover. Her love was virtuous, but in the end, it caused nothing but pain. Rot overtook the druid, and she is now a monstrous being that spreads and thrives on decay. The Beast of Blight moves effortlessly through decay and spews putrid spores when roused to anger.
Secret. Buried deep within the Beast of Blight, a tiny fragment of Farryn still exists. If shown an image of her lost love, the ignited memory will burn away the rot.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 544 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Brimstone Behemoth

trait.name: Burning Sacrifce
trait.text: If a creature willingly sacrifces its life in fire or lava for the purpose of defeating the behemoth, the creature can't be returned to life by any means. For the next 24 hours, the behemoth has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws, its speed is halved, it has vulnerability to radiant damage, and it loses its Legendary Resistance trait.

trait.name: Consuming Flame
trait.text: When the behemoth deals fire damage, it can choose to deal necrotic damage instead.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the behemoth fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The behemoth has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Siege Monster
trait.text: The behemoth deals double damage to objects and structures.

trait.name: Tunneler
trait.text: The behemoth can burrow through solid rock at half its burrowing speed and leaves a 20-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The behemoth makes one Bite attack, and it makes two Claw or Blazing Boulder attacks in any combination.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +18 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 26 (3d10 + 10) slashing damage plus 11 (2d10) fire damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 20). Until the grapple ends, the target is restrained.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +18 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (3d8 + 10) slashing damage plus 5 (1d10) fire damage.

action.name: Blazing Boulder
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +18 to hit, range 150 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (1d10 + 10) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) fire damage, and the target is knocked prone.

action.name: Cataclysmic Spellcasting (1/Day)
action.text: The Brimstone Behemoth casts Earthquake and Meteor Swarm, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 24).

reaction.name: Hellish Rebuke
reaction.text: When the behemoth is damaged by a creature it can see within 60 feet of it, it casts Hellish Rebuke on the creature, using the same spellcasting ability as Cataclysmic Spellcasting.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn, 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The behemoth can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The behemoth regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Fire Rain
legendary.text: The behemoth makes one Blazing Boulder attack.

legendary.name: Trample
legendary.text: The behemoth makes one Blazing Boulder attack.Trample. The behemoth moves up to half its speed, and it makes one Claw attack. On a hit, if the target is a creature, it is knocked prone.

legendary.name: Lava Well (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The behemoth causes lava to cover the ground in its space and in any unoccupied spaces in any tunnels it left by burrowing within 100 feet. A creature that touches the lava for the first time on a turn or starts its turn in the lava takes 55 (10d10) fire damage. A creature takes this damage only once per round. The lava cools and hardens after 1 hour or when the behemoth uses this action again.

legendary.name: The Brimstone Behemoth's Lair
legendary.text: The Brimstone Behemoth's lair is a vast underground cavern with magma, flames, and smoke. Once this colossus awakens, the crater that its cavern becomes serves as its lair. If encountered in its lair, the Brimstone Behemoth has a challenge rating of 26 (90,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Brimstone Behemoth's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Relentless Blaze. Within 10 miles of the lair, flames become driven to spread and consume. Every hour, contained fires in that area have a 10 percent chance of spreading to a flammable object within 10 feet. Un-contained fires spread out of control in 10 minutes.
Tenacious Lava. A creature that takes damage from lava within 1 mile of the lair ignites. Until a creature within 5 feet of the fire takes an action to douse the flames, the target takes 3 (1d6) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.
If the Brimstone Behemoth dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Apocalyptic Colossus of Hellfire
The emergence of the Brimstone Behemoth means armageddon for whatever realm it erupts into. A Fiend as old as time itself, it is made of hellfire, brimstone, and dying worlds. It has a malevolent will that seeks only to bring about mass extinction and apocalypse. The only hope for many civilizations has been through the regular willing sacrifice of citizens to slow or delay the inevitable doom to come.
Secret. If a soul willingly perishes in flame or lava to ensure the behemoth's demise, the Fiend will find its flame quenched.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 546 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Chained Reaper
monster.resist: acid, cold, fire, lightning, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained

trait.name: Ancestral Shame
trait.text: If a creature that can speak Orc within 30 feet of the reaper takes an action to read aloud the names carved in the stone on the reaper's back, the reaper takes 20 radiant damage.

trait.name: Ethereal Sight
trait.text: The reaper can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The reaper can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the reaper fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Wrapped Chains
trait.text: The reaper is wrapped in ghostly chains. It adds its Charisma modifier to its AC instead of Dexterity.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The reaper makes one Balefire Scythe attack and one Chain attack, and it uses either Chain Slam or Fling.

action.name: Balefire Scythe
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (2d8 + 5) necrotic damage, and the target begins burning with balefire. Until a creature within 5 feet of the fire takes an action to douse the flames, the target takes 2 (1d4) necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns.

action.name: Chain
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (2d10 + 5) piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 15) from one of four chains.

action.name: Chain Slam
action.text: The reaper slams creatures in its chains into each other or the environment. Each creature grappled by the reaper must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 9 (1d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage and is stunned until the start of the reaper's next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Etherealness
action.text: The reaper casts the Etherealness spell, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. The reaper is visible on the Material Plane while on the Border Ethereal and vice versa, but it can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.

action.name: Fling
action.text: The reaper throws a Large or smaller creature grappled by it to a space it can see within 30 feet of itself that isn't in the air. The thrown creature and each creature in the destination space must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 9 (2d8) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

legendary.name: Legendary Actions (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The reaper can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The reaper regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Drift
legendary.text: The reaper moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Lantern Flare
legendary.text: The reaper's lantern fares with balefire. Each creature of the reaper's choice within 20 feet of it must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or have disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls until the end of its next turn.

legendary.name: Balefire Scythe (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The reaper makes one Balefire Scythe attack.

legendary.name: The Chained Reaper's Lair
legendary.text: The Chained Reaper's lair is a mausoleum holding its own empty grave and those of its former companions.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Chained Reaper's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Chained Vitality. A creature that isn't a Construct or Undead that finishes a long rest within 1 mile of the lair must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have its hit point maximum reduced by 5 (to a minimum of 1 HP) until it next finishes a long rest.
Falling Shroud. Thin mist arises within 1 mile of the lair. Undead with the Incorporeal Movement trait in that area have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
If the Chained Reaper dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Burdened Harbinger of Death
The Chained Reaper is all that remains of a once-noble orc caretaker of the departed called Yorgrim. Having been corrupted by the mists of death known as the Shroud, the Chained Reaper is a being of hatred and bitterness, striving to cremate all living things in its balefire before binding them in chains of anguish.
Secret. The Chained Reaper carries a memorial stone upon its back. If one can read aloud the names inscribed there, their purity will burn away the reaper's fury.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 548 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Crimson Abbot

trait.name: Honor Lost
trait.text: If a creature takes a bonus action to present a memento or recite a reminder of the abbot's past and succeeds on a DC 15 Charisma (Persuasion) check, the abbot has disadvantage on the next ability check, attack roll, or saving throw he makes before the start of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the abbot fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The abbot can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sunlight Hypersensitivity
trait.text: The abbot takes 20 radiant damage if he starts his turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, he has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The abbot makes two Sanguine Blade attacks. He can replace one attack with a use of Blood Call.

action.name: Sanguine Blade
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d10 + 6) slashing damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Blood Call
action.text: One creature other than a Construct or Undead within 30 feet of the abbot must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 7 (2d6) necrotic damage and its hit point maximum is reduced by the damage taken until it finishes a long rest. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. The abbot regains hit points equal to the damage taken. A Humanoid reduced to 0 hit points by this damage and then buried rises the following sunset as a Crimson Clergy under the abbot's control.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The abbot casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 15):
• At Will: Command, Disguise Self (can appear as a Medium Humanoid, 24-hour duration), Thaumaturgy • 1/day: Word of Recall (to lair only)

reaction.name: Parry
reaction.text: When the abbot is hit by an attack roll while holding a weapon, he adds 3 to his AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

legendary.name: Legendary Actions (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The abbot can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The abbot regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

legendary.name: Castigate
legendary.text: One creature the abbot can see within 60 feet of himself must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

legendary.name: Bloody Strike (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The abbot moves up to half his speed and makes one Sanguine Blade attack.

legendary.name: Overwhelm (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The abbot casts Command at 2nd level.

legendary.name: The Crimson Abbot's Lair
legendary.text: The Crimson Abbot's lair is a blood-soaked monastery where unworthy mortals are burned in sacrifice, and vampire minions study truths hidden in blood.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Crimson Abbot's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Fresh Blood. Within 1 mile of the lair, spilled blood doesn't dry out or coagulate, instead remaining fresh for 24 hours.
Incinerate Heresy. Within 1 mile of the lair, when an enemy of the abbot's takes fire damage, that creature starts burning, taking 2 (1d4) damage at the start of its next turn.
If the Crimson Abbot dies or moves his lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.spells: Command, Disguise Self, Thaumaturgy, Word of Recall
monster.description:
Unholy Crusader of Blood
In life, the Crimson Abbot was a virtuous crusader named Sir Marius Renathyr but was seduced by the sweet whispers of an archdevil of deceit and pride who masqueraded as a goddess of righteousness. On the brink of death, the abbot accepted the whispered offer for eternal life, and in so doing, was transformed into a creature of unbearable pride with an unquenchable thirst for blood. He continues to work in the name of his "goddess" and has gathered a fanatically loyal congregation eager for his promise of pure, everlasting life.
Secret. The Crimson Abbot's pride is nearly unshakable, but seeing a true reminder of the righteous man he once was can crack his façade.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 550 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Dusk Mother
monster.resist: acid, cold, fire, lightning, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained, stunned

trait.name: Death's Shroud
trait.text: The mother is always invisible. Blindsight, truesight, and other effects that allow a creature to see invisible creatures don't reveal the mother. A creature that has been reduced to 0 hit points at least once in its life can see her normally.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The mother can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. She takes 5 (1d10) force damage if she ends her turn inside an object.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the mother fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The mother has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Steadfast Matron
trait.text: Undead creatures of the mother's choice within 60 feet of her (excluding herself) have advantage on saving throws. She can't use this trait if she is incapacitated.

trait.name: Undeniable Guilt
trait.text: At the start of her turn, if the mother can see a possession once owned by her living children, or if she can see or hear the spirits of her children, she becomes incapacitated until the start of her next turn, and she must move as far from the object or spirits as possible.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The mother makes three Shears attacks.

action.name: Shears
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 25 (4d8 + 7) force damage. If the target is a creature and is hit with two or more Shears attacks in the same turn, it gains 1 level of exhaustion.

action.name: Mournful Tolling (1/Day)
