action.text: The mother focuses her wail into her bell. Each creature within 30 feet of the mother must make a DC 22 Wisdom saving throw. A Construct, an Undead, or a creature that has ever been raised from the dead is unaffected. On a failed save, a target takes 77 (14d10) psychic damage that ignores damage resistance, and the target becomes stunned until the end of the mother's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Soul Doll (Bonus Action)
action.text: The mother targets the corpse of a creature she killed within the last minute. The target's soul is drawn out and transforms into a ceramic doll of the creature. The doll is a Tiny object with AC 15, HP equal to half the creature's maximum hit points, vulnerability to thunder damage, and immunity to poison damage. The soul can dimly see and hear its surroundings, and creatures with telepathy can communicate with the soul. If the doll is destroyed, the soul arises from the doll as a Shadow under the mother's control. The creature can't be returned to life unless its soul is fired from the doll by a Wish spell, or by destroying the shadow it spawned.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn, 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The mother can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The mother regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

legendary.name: Drift
legendary.text: The mother moves up to her speed without provoking opportunity attacks. When she enters a creature's space during this move, that creature takes 16 (3d10) cold damage and has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.

legendary.name: Snip
legendary.text: The mother makes one Shears attack. On a hit, the target takes an extra 4 (1d8) necrotic damage.

legendary.name: Endless Keening (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: Each creature within 30 feet of the mother that can hear her must make a DC 22 Constitution saving throw. A Construct, an Undead, or a creature that has ever been raised from the dead is unaffected. On a failed save, a target takes 28 (8d6) necrotic damage that ignores damage resistance, if any. A target that fails by 5 or more is reduced to 0 hit points. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage.

legendary.name: The Dusk Mother's Lair
legendary.text: The Dusk Mother's lair is a home or other place in a settlement where tragedy struck, most often those involving children or families broken by loss. If encountered in her lair, the Dusk Mother has a challenge rating of 25 (75,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Dusk Mother's lair is changed by her presence, creating the following effects:
Enduring Dirge. Within 1 mile of the lair, creatures with an Intelligence score higher than 4 who can speak at least one language absently sing nursery rhymes or funeral songs without intending to.
Thoughts of Loss. Whenever a living creature within 1 mile of the lair finishes a short rest, it must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or it gains no benefit from the rest.
If the Dusk Mother dies or moves her lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Shade of Motherly Anguish
The keening wail of a mother is unmistakable and haunting, and the legends that tell of the Dusk Mother barely scratch the surface of her terrifying existence. She carries a pair of oversized shears and a great bell she tolls to accompany her wailing. Her eternal torment stems from her children, who met their fate due to the Dusk Mother's negligence—or perhaps her wrath. Now she cries in shame for that loss as she searches for her children, or for souls to claim and fashion into dolls in a futile attempt to fill the void in her doomed soul.
Secret. The Dusk Mother's only true weakness or fear is the remnants of her children-some trinket or toy they played with in life or, more chillingly, their spirits, which also wander the world, but never in the same place as their mother's shade.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 552 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Galloping Headsman
monster.vulnerable: damage dealt by a creature holding the headman's original skull

trait.name: Binding Skull
trait.text: The headsman has vulnerability to any damage dealt by a creature holding the headsman's original skull, and it has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls made against that creature.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the headsman fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The headsman has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The headsman makes two Headsman's Axe attacks and one Hooves attack.

action.name: Headsman's Axe
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 19 (2d12 + 6) slashing damage plus 16 (3d10) fire damage. If the target is a creature with at least one head and the headsman rolled a 20 on the attack roll, the target must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or it is decapitated and dies if it can't survive without a head. A target is immune to this effect if it takes none of the damage, has Legendary Actions, or is Huge or larger. Such a creature takes an extra 28 (8d6) force damage from the hit.

action.name: Hooves
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (4d8 + 6) bludgeoning damage. If the headsman moved at least 20 feet immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) necrotic damage and is knocked prone.

action.name: Flaming Skull (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The headsman hurls its skull in a 90-foot-long, 10-foot-wide line. The skull then reappears in the headsman's hand. Each creature in that line must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw, taking 63 (14d8) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A target that takes damage from the skull ignites. Until a creature within 5 feet of the target takes an action to douse the flames, the target takes 2 (1d4) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn or 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The headsman can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The headsman regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Move
legendary.text: The headsman moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Rancorous Gaze
legendary.text: One creature the headsman can see within 90 feet of itself must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

legendary.name: Relentless Execution (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The headsman makes one Headsman's Axe attack with advantage. If the target is frightened, treat a 19 on the d20 as a 20.

legendary.name: The Galloping Headsman's Lair
legendary.text: The Galloping Headsman's lair is the stretch of road that serves as the anchor point of the creature's search for vengeance. If encountered in his lair, the Galloping Headsman has a challenge rating of 19 (22,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Galloping Headsman's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Encroaching Doom. Within 6 miles of the lair, an air of doom clings to creatures. Creatures have disadvantage on saving throws made to avoid or end the frightened condition that aren't caused by the Galloping Headsman.
Interminable Journey. Paths warp and stretch within 6 miles of the lair. In that area, travel pace outside of combat is halved.
If the Galloping Headsman dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Headless Executioner of Vengeance
The Galloping Headsman is a formidable combination of human and horse musculature fused with nightmarish magic and dark revenge. Gouts of flame burst from where its head and tail should be and dance dangerously along the edge of its massive two-headed axe. A burning, skull-shaped pumpkin floats eerily in one hand, its fiery visage a constantly shifting train of agonized faces.
Secret. The Galloping Headsman's original skull is its greatest weakness, and anyone holding the skull becomes the apparition's bane.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 554 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Ghostlight Locomotive

trait.name: Crooked Switch (1/Day)
trait.text: When the locomotive has half its hit points or fewer, it regains all expended uses of Legendary Resistance and enters a malevolent form for 12 hours. In this form, it is chaotic evil and can use its Multiattack action.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
trait.text: If the locomotive fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack (Crooked Switch Only)
action.text: The locomotive makes two Rail Slam attacks.

action.name: Rail Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) bludgeoning damage, and the target is pushed up to 10 feet straight away.

action.name: Spirit Exhaust
action.text: The locomotive spews spiritual smoke in a 30-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (2/Turn)
legendary.text: The locomotive can take 2 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The locomotive regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Ghostly Rails
legendary.text: The locomotive moves up to half its speed in a straight line without provoking opportunity attacks. A Large or smaller creature whose space it passes through is grappled (escape DC 14) from the spectral rails.

legendary.name: Haunting Whistle (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The locomotive blows its wailing steam whistle. One creature within 30 feet that can hear the whistle must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or take 4 (1d8) psychic damage and be frightened until the end of its next turn.

legendary.name: The Ghostlight Locomotive's Lair
legendary.text: The Ghostlight Locomotive's lair is a 1-mile-radius shrouding mist originating from the locomotive. This mist doesn't extend within the cars of the Ghostlight Express that the locomotive pulls.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Ghostlight Locomotive's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Ghastly Shroud. When a creature that isn't Undead finishes a short or long rest within 1 mile of the locomotive, the creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or any time it regains hit points, it only regains half the amount. This effect ends when the creature next finishes a long rest or receives a Lesser Restoration spell or similar magic.
Spirit Calling. Within 1 mile of the locomotive, hostile Shrouded* and Specters are abundant and drawn to any creature that attacks the Ghostlight Locomotive or that tries to keep pace with, climb, or enter the Ghostlight Express.
If the Ghostlight Locomotive dies, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Corrupted Ghost Train
The Ghostlight Locomotive is the engine of a phantom train that travels unseen between the realms of life and death, ferrying souls of the dead to their afterlife. During its normal operation, the locomotive burns doomed spirits for fuel and races along spectral, misty rails that appear as it travels, and it has little awareness beyond its crucial task. Only a drastic event, such as the corruption of its soul-made fuel source, can turn the Ghostlight Locomotive from its endless duty, whereupon it stokes a consciousness of the innumerable souls it has burned and vents the fury of the damned upon any obstacles in its path. When pressed, the residue of wicked souls left from the burning of its corrupted fuel overwhelms the engine's amoral nature, driving it to malevolent and hateful destruction.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 556 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Grinning Sinner

trait.name: Coffin Rider
trait.text: The sinner has a magic coffin that has immunity to all damage. While standing on the coffin, the sinner rides it on a ghostly wave of swamp water with a speed of 60 feet and can move over liquids as if they were solid.

trait.name: Curse Bottle
trait.text: The sinner has a bottle that holds cursed magic. The bottle has AC 15, HP 25, and immunity to all damage except force. If the bottle is destroyed, the sinner can magically create a new one when he finishes a long rest.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the sinner fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Overweening Pride
trait.text: As a bonus action, a creature can insult the sinner's skill and renown as a pirate captain. The sinner must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 10 psychic damage and have disadvantage on attack rolls against any target other than the creature.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The sinner makes two attacks, using Hex Grasp or Blunderbuss in any combination. He can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast Bane.

action.name: Hex Grasp
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it is cursed until the start of the sinner's next turn, or until the sinner curses it in this way again. While cursed, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls and on ability checks that rely on an ability of the sinner's choice.

action.name: Blunderbuss
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (3d8 + 5) force damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is pushed up to 10 feet straight away.

action.name: Curse Blast (Recharge 5-6)
action.text:
The sinner unleashes one of the following curses from his blunderbuss:
Jack's Anguish. A magenta wave fires out with a deep, wailing moan. Each creature in a 30-foot cone must make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 11 (2d10) psychic damage and is frightened for 1 minute. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. A frightened creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Shadowy Friends. A wave of shadow coughs forth and wraps around foes in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or become paralyzed until the end of the sinner's next turn. If a paralyzed target takes damage, the condition ends early.

action.name: Spellcasting (Requires Curse Bottle)
action.text:
The sinner casts one of the following spells using his Curse Bottle (and no other material components) and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17):
• At Will: Bane, Bestow Curse, Major Image, Remove Curse • 1/day Each: Animate Dead, Creation, Darkness

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The sinner can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The sinner regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

legendary.name: Barbed Curse
legendary.text: The sinner targets one creature cursed by him or affected by his Bane spell within 30 feet of himself. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or take 6 (1d12) force damage.

legendary.name: Jinxed Ground
legendary.text: Cursed magenta symbols flash on the ground within 20 feet of the sinner. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or be knocked prone.

legendary.name: Grinning Grasp (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The sinner teleports to an unoccupied space he can see within 30 feet. He makes one Hex Grasp attack before or after teleporting and can't take this action again until the start of his next turn.

legendary.name: The Grinning Sinner's Lair
legendary.text: The Grinning Sinner's lair is the derelict riverboat casino, the Dead Man's Hand.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Grinning Sinner's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Fate's Wheel. Within 1 mile of the lair, if the number rolled on a die to restore hit points is even, increase the number rolled by 1. If the number rolled is odd, reduce it by 1.
Run of Bad Luck. Within 1 mile of the lair, a creature that rolls a 1 on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw must subtract 1d4 from its next roll that isn't a damage roll.
If the Grinning Sinner dies or moves his lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.spells: Bane, Bestow Curse, Major Image, Remove Curse, Animate Dead, Creation, Darkness
monster.description:
Dealer of Jinxes
Once the dashing pirate captain Briggsy Kratch, the crocodile-folk now known as the Grinning Sinner made a bargain for power with a spirit known as Snake Eye Jack. The sinner grew discontent with the power he was gifted and tricked Jack into a coffin before sealing him inside. Now the Grinning Sinner siphons his trapped patron's cursed power and makes bargains of his own to snare the souls of the desperate and foolish.
Secret. The Grinning Sinner holds his prowess and reputation as a fearsome and successful pirate captain above all; causing him to doubt his renown can turn his hexed magic against him.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 558 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Harvest Terror
monster.conditionImmune: charmed (except for its Unraveling Song trait), exhaustion, frightened, poisoned

trait.name: Demonic Mimicry
trait.text: The terror can mimic voices and sounds it has heard. A listener can discern the mimicry for what it is with a successful DC 18 Wisdom (Insight) check.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the terror fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The terror has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Unraveling Song
trait.text: As an action, a creature can play or sing Jericho Sticks' favorite song, "Coal-Eyed Birds," and the terror is charmed until the end of the creature's next turn. The charmed condition ends early if the creature damages the terror or forces it to make a saving throw.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The terror makes one Claw attack and one Scythe attack.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is knocked prone.

action.name: Scythe
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) psychic damage.

action.name: Dirge of Secrets (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The terror shrieks a chorus of horrific secrets. Each creature within 30 feet of the terror must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 11 (2d10) psychic damage and has disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. An affected creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The terror can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The terror regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Averting Winds
legendary.text: The terror casts the Wind Wall spell (which can't appear in a creature's space), requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

legendary.name: Crow's Swoop
legendary.text: The terror moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Devour Secrets (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The terror's beak gapes and devours secrets. Each creature within 20 feet of the terror must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw (with disadvantage if the target has given up one of its secrets to the terror). On a failed save, a target takes 7 (2d6) psychic damage, and its speed is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

legendary.name: The Harvest Terror's Lair
legendary.text: The Harvest Terror's lair is the extraplanar corn maze known as the Fields of the Crow.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Harvest Terror's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Amber Eyes. Within 1 mile of the lair, amber-eyed crows watch everything. Enemies of the Harvest Terror have disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks in the region.
Spilled Secrets. Within 1 mile of the lair, creatures other than the Harvest Terror have disadvantage on Charisma (Deception) checks made to keep secrets.If the Harvest Terror dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Demon of Secrets and Despair
Once a jovial soul imbued into the body of a scarecrow called Ol' Jericho Sticks, the Harvest Terror is now a nightmarish abomination. It is a mockery of demonic flesh molded in the shape of a crow-like scarecrow. Its twisted body is made of molding wicker with four tattered, black-feathered wings sprouting from its back. An oversized corvid head with amber eyes sits upon its broad shoulders, its dark beak a jagged mess, and it wields a chipped, wicked scythe covered in old blood. Forever hungry, the Harvest Terror feeds on secrets and those that keep them.
Secret. The soul of Ol' Jericho Sticks still exists in the dark recesses of the Harvest Terror's mind. Singing or playing Jericho's favorite song tips the balance of control over Jericho's stolen body.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 560 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Horned King

trait.name: Enduring Restoration
trait.text: If the Horned King dies, its body disappears in a cloud of ash. It becomes an intangible, invisible spirit in its home forest, and it can't reform a new physical body for 10d10 years.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (5/Day)
trait.text: If the Horned King fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The Horned King has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The Horned King makes three Cursed Strike attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Terrorizing Breath.

action.name: Cursed Strike
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit (against a cursed target, scores a critical hit on a 19 or 20 on the d20), reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (Horned King's choice) plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it is cursed until the end of the Horned King's next turn. While cursed, it must subtract 1d4 from ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws.

action.name: Breath of Corruption (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The Horned King breathes sweet-smelling shadow in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 21 (6d6) necrotic damage plus 21 (6d6) psychic damage, and it gains 1 level of exhaustion that ends at the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Terrorizing Breath
action.text: The Horned King breathes bitter shadow in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 19 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for 1 minute. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Sense Desire (Bonus Action)
action.text: The Horned King casts Detect Thoughts, requiring no spell components and using Charisma for the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 20).

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The Horned King can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The Horned King regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Taste of Wickedness
legendary.text: The Horned King's snakelike tongue whips out hungrily at a cursed creature within 15 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Charisma saving throw or take 16 (3d10) psychic damage. The Horned King regains hit points equal to the damage taken, and one curse of the Horned King's choice afecting the target ends.

legendary.name: Temptation
legendary.text: The Horned King speaks to a creature it can see within 120 feet and offers to either restore 20 of its hit points or grant it inspiration. If the creature accepts, it gains that benefit but is cursed for 1 minute. While cursed, the Horned King has resistance to all damage the target deals.

legendary.name: Trample (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The Horned King moves up to half its speed, and it makes one Cursed Strike attack.

legendary.name: The Horned King's Lair
legendary.text: The Horned King's lair is the forest clearing where a twisted tree focuses the malevolent will that gave rise to the king's physical presence.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Horned King's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Sweet Temptation. Within 1 mile of the lair, a creature that acts on a sinful impulse to the detriment of a friendly or indifferent creature increases its hit point maximum and current hit points by 10 until it finishes a long rest. Creatures are aware of this effect. This can't occur again for that creature until it finishes a long rest.
Tenacious Curses. Within 1 mile of the lair, saving throws against curses have disadvantage.
If the Horned King dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Sin Made Manifest
Every sinful, selfish action in the world pours a fraction of itself into the Horned King, a being composed of every mortal who has surrendered to temptation. Fortunately, under normal circumstances, the Horned King exists as an ephemeral spirit with no physical presence. During times of upheaval, when a great many mortals give in to their selfish desires or when another power—such as a deity, demon lord, or archfey—interferes, the Horned King is made manifest, and with it comes the weight of sin fooding back into the world that created it.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 562 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Jinxed Leviathan

trait.name: Amphibious
trait.text: The leviathan can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the leviathan fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Returned Curse
trait.text: If the leviathan curses a creature holding an idol of Snake Eye Jack blessed by one of his devoted followers, the creature is unaffected by the curse. The leviathan then takes 20 radiant damage and has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The leviathan makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (2d12 + 10) piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) force damage. The target is grappled (escape DC 20), and until the grapple ends, the target is restrained.

action.name: Tail
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 23 (3d8 + 10) bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it is knocked prone and is pushed up to 20 feet straight away from the leviathan.

action.name: Accursed Breath (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The leviathan breathes magenta light in a 120-foot-long, 10-foot-wide line. Each creature in that area must make a DC 22 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 78 (12d12) necrotic damage and is cursed. The cursed target has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws, and it can't regain hit points. The target can end the curse early if it voluntarily fails a saving throw forced by one of its enemies. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Swallow
action.text: One Large or smaller creature grappled by the leviathan must make a DC 25 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it is swallowed and no longer grappled. A swallowed creature is blinded and restrained, it has total cover against attacks and other effects outside the leviathan, and it takes 24 (7d6) force damage at the start of each of the leviathan's turns. The leviathan can have up to four creatures swallowed at a time. If the leviathan takes 50 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the leviathan must succeed on a DC 25 Constitution saving throw at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 10 feet of the leviathan and is knocked prone. If the leviathan dies, any swallowed creature is no longer restrained and can escape from the corpse using 15 feet of movement, exiting prone.

reaction.name: Jinxed Hide
reaction.text: When a creature within 60 feet of the leviathan hits it with an attack roll or targets it with a spell, the creature must succeed on a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw or become cursed until the end of its next turn. The cursed creature has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws and can't regain hit points.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn, 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The leviathan can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The leviathan regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Devour
legendary.text: The leviathan uses Swallow.

legendary.name: Swamp Surge
legendary.text: The leviathan causes a swell of muddy water and grasping roots to fll the area within 20 feet of it. Each creature within that area must make a DC 20 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 21 (6d6) bludgeoning damage, and its speed is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

legendary.name: Tail Sweep
legendary.text: The leviathan moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks and makes one Tail attack.

legendary.name: The Jinxed Leviathan's Lair
legendary.text: The Jinxed Leviathan's lair is a cursed swamp steeped in confounding magic. If encountered in its lair, the Jinxed Leviathan has a challenge rating of 24 (62,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Jinxed Leviathan's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Hanging Hex. Within 1 mile of the lair, when a creature other than the Jinxed Leviathan rolls a 1 or a 20 on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, the next ability check, attack roll, or saving throw the creature makes has disadvantage, and it can't have advantage.
Murky waters. Within 1 mile of the lair, swamp water is cloudy and shows fleeting faces in the murk. Creatures in the water have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
If the Jinxed Leviathan dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Curse-Wrought Swamp Monster
Once a normal alligator that dwelled in a cursed swamp, the Jinxed Leviathan was warped by the strange hexes and twisted spells of nefarious magicians who sought to defeat the deathly spirit known as Snake Eye Jack. Growing to colossal proportions and infusing with the essence of misfortune itself, the Monstrosity could not be controlled and devoured its makers. Now, it lurks in the still, black water of hexed bayous, always seeking its next meal, upon which it unleashes its jinxcraft before feasting.
Secret. An idol of the fabled deal maker Snake Eye Jack that has been blessed by one of Jack's followers can turn the Jinxed Leviathan's own cursed power against it.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 564 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Lord of Fools

trait.name: Bitter Medicine
trait.text: As a bonus action, a creature can dismiss or belittle the lord's attempts at humor. If the creature succeeds on a DC 16 Charisma (Deception) check, the lord has disadvantage on attack rolls against any target other than that creature until the start of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Infinite Jest
trait.text: The lord creates an illusion of a Small Humanoid jester in his space. While he isn't incapacitated, the lord can move the image up to 60 feet during his turn (no action required). The lord can perceive and speak through this image as if he were in the image's space. The image is insubstantial, but it can manipulate objects as if it has a Strength of 10.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the lord fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Reactive
trait.text: The lord can take one reaction on every turn of combat.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The lord makes two attacks, using Ribbon or Joy Buzzer in any combination. If the lord has cast Project Image, he can make melee Joy Buzzer attacks from the image's space.

action.name: Ribbon
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 15) from one of six ribbons and restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Joy Buzzer
action.text: The lord's Infinite Jest illusion moves to a target within range. Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 24 (3d12 + 5) lightning damage, and the target can't take reactions until the end of the lord's next turn. Hit or Miss: The jester vanishes and reappears in the lord's space.

action.name: Exploding Pie (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: The lord throws a massive, corrosive custard pie. The pie explodes in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point the lord can see within 100 feet of himself. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 16 (3d10) acid damage and is blinded until the end of the lord's next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The lord casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 19):
• At Will: Hideous Laughter, Project Image (appears as a Small Humanoid jester), Vicious Mockery (4d6 psychic damage) • 1/day Each: Irresistible Dance, Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion

action.name: Ribbon Step (Bonus Action)
action.text: The lord vanishes in a burst of confetti and ribbons and teleports to an unoccupied space he can see within 30 feet.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The lord can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The lord regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

legendary.name: Frolic
legendary.text: The lord moves up to half his speed without provoking opportunity attacks, during which he can move through other creature's spaces without expending extra movement to do so. A creature whose space the lord enters takes 7 (2d6) psychic damage. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.

legendary.name: Joke's on You
legendary.text: The lord uses Spellcasting to cast either Irresistible Dance or Vicious Mockery.

legendary.name: Ribbon Dance (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: Each creature the lord is grappling must make a DC 18 Strength saving throw, taking 22 (4d10) bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: The Lord of Fool's Lair
legendary.text: The Lord of Fools' lair is the Fool's Revel carnival.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Lord of Fools' lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Empty Treats. Within 1 mile of the lair, carnival snack food is abundant even in unlikely places. The food is delicious but offers no sustenance. A creature that eats the food can't feel the effects of starvation (though they still suffer the effects of going without food) for 24 hours.
Pratfalls. Within 1 mile of the lair, ability checks or saving throws made to avoid being knocked prone have disadvantage.
If the Lord of Fools dies or moves his lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.spells: Tasha's Hideous Laughter, Project Image, Vicious Mockery, Irresistible Dance, Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion
monster.description:
Fiendish Master of Revels
The Lord of Fools is the embodiment of revelry, cherishing amusement at any cost. He most often leads a procession of revelers of song, revelers of stories, and heralds of fools, compelling townsfolk and travelers to join their celebration to the exclusion of all other concerns. Many of these celebrants dance, drink, and make merry until they drop—sometimes dead! The Lord of Fools laughs all the louder and longer at their demise.
Secret. The Lord of Fools thrives on laughter and amusement, so when someone doesn't react accordingly to one of his japes, he becomes enraged at that creature to the exclusion of others.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 566 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Tall Man

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day, or 5/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the Tall Man fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The Tall Man has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Memory's Dance
trait.text: As a bonus action, a creature can perform some steps from a dance of the Tall Man's court and make a DC 20 Charisma (Performance) check. On a success, the Tall Man takes 20 force damage and has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws until the start of the creature's next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The Tall Man makes two Claw attacks and uses Spellcasting to cast Vicious Mockery (4d6 psychic damage).

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) psychic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 14) from one of two claws and restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The Tall Man casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 19):
• At Will: Invisibility, Minor Illusion, Vicious Mockery (4d6 psychic damage) • 2/day: Darkness

action.name: Elusive Disguise (Bonus Action)
action.text: The Tall Man magically transforms into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or back to his true form. His statistics, other than his size, are the same in each form. Any equipment he is wearing or carrying changes to accommodate his new form. He can take on any features he chooses, but he is always unnaturally tall for whatever guise he takes. He returns to his true form if he dies.

reaction.name: Evasive Blink
reaction.text: When the Tall Man takes damage, he gains resistance to the triggering damage and teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space he can see.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn, 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The Tall Man can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The Tall Man regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

legendary.name: Spellcasting
legendary.text: The Tall Man uses Spellcasting

legendary.name: Teleport
legendary.text: The Tall Man teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space he can see. When he teleports, he can force a creature he is grappling to succeed on a DC 18 Charisma saving throw or teleport with him, appearing in the closest unoccupied space to the Tall Man's destination.

legendary.name: Strike (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The Tall Man makes one Claw attack.

legendary.name: The Tall Man's Lair
legendary.text: The Tall Man's lair is the dark forest he now haunts, where his forgotten Fey court once thrived. If encountered in his lair, the Tall Man has a challenge rating of 21 (33,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Tall Man's lair is changed by his presence, creating the following effects:
Deepening Shadows. Within 1 mile of the lair, shadows stretch far beyond what they should. Creatures can find a sufficiently shadowy spot to hide in, even without cover, 50 percent of the time.
Too Quiet. Sounds are muffled within 1 mile of the lair, halving the distance at which sounds can be heard.
If the Tall Man dies or moves his lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.spells: Invisibility, Minor Illusion, Vicious Mockery, Darkness
monster.description:
Murderous Archfey of the Dark Wood
The Tall Man is a mysterious entity that haunts an accursed forest. Once, he was an archfey who ruled a now-forgotten court, renowned for its dances that lasted for nights on end. Doomed now to walk his rotting forest, the Tall Man finds pleasure where he can by tempting travelers and forest denizens alike to play hide and seek with him—with deadly results.
Secret. Dancing forgotten steps once performed in the fey court that the Tall Man formerly ruled shreds his being.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 568 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Vermintoll Abomination
monster.languages: Common, Deep Speech, Infernal, Primordial (Auran), Sylvan

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day)
trait.text: If the abomination fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The abomination has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
trait.text: The abomination has three heads: a centipede hag's, a pigeon hag's, and a weasel hag's. Whenever the abomination takes 50 damage or more on a single turn, one of its heads dies (determined randomly). The abomination dies if all its heads are dead. At the end of each of its turns, if it has at least 1 hit point and any of its heads are dead, the abomination regains 20 hit points and one of its dead heads returns to life.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The abomination makes three attacks, using Cauldron Slam or Brew Spray in any combination.

action.name: Cauldron Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) acid damage.

action.name: Brew Spray
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 90 ft., one target. Hit: 20 (4d8 + 2) acid damage, and the target takes a −2 penalty to AC until the end of the abomination's next turn.

action.name: Spellcasting
action.text:
The abomination casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18):
• At Will: Dispel Magic, Hypnotic Pattern • 1/day Each: Contagion, Dream, Dominate Person

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The abomination can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The abomination regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Corrosive Teleport
legendary.text: The abomination teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see. Each creature within 10 feet of the abomination when it appears must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 7 (2d6) acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Hag's Head
legendary.text:
The abomination uses one of the following options (if the necessary head is alive); it can't use that option again until the start of its next turn:
• Contagious Miasma (Centipede Hag Head). One creature the abomination can see within 5 feet of it must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 17 (5d6) poison damage, is poisoned, and can't regain hit points while poisoned in this way. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A poisoned creature repeats the saving throw when it finish a long rest, ending the effect on itself on a success.
• Overwhelming Doubt (Pigeon Hag Head). One creature the abomination can see within 30 feet it must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 13 (3d8) psychic damage and is incapacitated until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
• Waking Nightmare (Weasel Hag Head). One creature the abomination can see within 30 feet of it must make a DC 18 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 13 (2d12) psychic damage and is frightened for 1 minute. While frightened, the target perceives its allies as enemies but isn't compelled to act in a specific way. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. A frightened creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

legendary.name: Crushing Leap (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The abomination jumps to a space within 30 feet that contains one or more Large or smaller creatures. Each creature in the destination space must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 17 (2d12 + 4) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage, and the target is pushed 5 feet straight away from the abomination.

legendary.name: The Vermintoll Abomination's Lair
legendary.text: The Vermintoll Abomination's lair is a decrepit hideaway, often hidden in plain sight such as an attic, basement, or a seized home.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Vermintoll Abomination's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Endless Vermin. Within 1 mile of the lair, centipedes, weasels, (including giant varieties of both), and pigeons (use the Raven stat block) are common nuisances. They steal Tiny objects and foul food.
Loyal Dwelling. The lair itself, including any greater structure or complex it resides within, rejects intruders. Ability checks to pick locks or find secret doors have disadvantage.
If the Vermintoll Abomination dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.spells: Dispel Magic, Hypnotic Pattern, Contagion, Dream, Dominate Person
monster.description:
Verminous Coven Amalgamation
The magic that binds hag covens together is far more powerful than many spellcasters can fully comprehend. Vermintoll abominations come about when the magics of a centipede hag, a pigeon hag, and a weasel hag enmesh and fuse the witches into one mass, bound to their cauldron.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 570 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Weeping Widow

trait.name: Calculating Defense
trait.text: The widow adds her Intelligence modifier to AC instead of Dexterity.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the widow fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Reopened Memories
trait.text: At the start of her turn, if the widow can see a memento of her husband or some other reminder that forces her to realize what she's lost, she has disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws and can only expend 2 uses of her Legendary Actions until the start of her next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The widow makes two Injection attacks.

action.name: Injection
action.text:
Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 1 piercing damage plus 10 (3d6) poison damage. If the target is a creature, it's poisoned until the end of its next turn. While poisoned, the target suffers one of the following effects of the widow's choice (each option can only be used once per turn):
Hyperphobic Stimulant. The target becomes frightened. At the start of its turn, the target must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or take the Dash action and move away from the widow by the safest and shortest available route, unless there is nowhere to move.
Soporific Infusion. The target must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or its speed is halved, and on its turn, it can take either an action or a bonus action, but not both, and it can make only one attack if it takes the Attack action. A target that fails the save by 5 or more falls unconscious for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until a creature takes an action to shake it awake.
Thaumaturgic Inhibitor. If the target casts a spell, it must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or the spell fails, but a spell slot used to cast the spell isn't expended.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The widow can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The widow regains spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.

legendary.name: Alacrity
legendary.text: The widow or a creature she is riding moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Hypertrophic Injections
legendary.text:
The widow targets herself or an ally within 5 feet and grants the target one of the following effects of her choice; she can't target the same ally again until the start of her next turn:
• Aggression. The target has advantage on attack rolls until the end of its next turn.
• Fortitude. One of the following conditions of the widow's choice ends on the target: charmed, frightened, grappled, paralyzed, poisoned, restrained, stunned.
• Vigor. The target regains 10 hit points.

legendary.name: Dose (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The widow makes one Injection attack.

legendary.name: The Weeping Widow's Lair
legendary.text: The Weeping Widow's lair is the sanatorium where she conducts her horrific experiments.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Weeping Widow's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Dulled Memories. Within 1 mile of the lair, creatures have disadvantage on Intelligence checks made to recall information related to painful or emotional memories.
Faded Dreams. Within 1 mile of the lair, creatures who sleep or trance have only vague impressions of dreams and recall no details.
If the Weeping Widow dies or moves her lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Matron of Malevolent Medicine
Once a priestess committed to healing the sick through divine rites, the Weeping Widow abandoned the gods after a personal tragedy and turned to sinister science. Believing she knows how to best treat the suffering and make them stronger, she employs a harrowing regimen of alchemical and medical experimentation to wipe minds and alter bodies, whether the patient wants the treatment or not.
Secret. The only way to force the Weeping Widow to live up to her moniker is to remind her of the family she lost, such as her husband's sword or portrait, which forces her to confront what she has become.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 572 (Third Party)

monster.name: The White Worm
monster.languages: understands all but can't speak, Telepathy 120 ft.

trait.name: Inscrutable Mind
trait.text: If a creature tries to read the worm's mind, that creature must succeed on a DC 19 Intelligence saving throw or be stunned for 1 minute. The creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the worm fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Poisoned Knowledge
trait.text: If the worm uses Devour Mind on a creature that knows a special sequence of constellations and their path through the sky, at the start of the target's next turn, the worm takes 50 psychic damage and Devour Mind ends.

trait.name: Tunneler
trait.text: The worm can burrow through solid rock at half its burrowing speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The worm makes one Bite attack and uses Psychic Caress twice.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 10 ft.; one target. Hit: 25 (3d10 + 9) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) force damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 19).

action.name: Psychic Caress
action.text: A creature the worm can see within 60 feet of it must make a DC 19 Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 17 (5d6) psychic damage, and it is frightened until the start of the worm's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Devour Mind (1/Day)
action.text:
The worm vanishes and magically enters the mind of a creature it can see within 60 feet of it. The worm can't target a creature under the effect of the Mind Blank or Protection from Evil and Good spells. While in the target's mind, the worm can't take actions, and the target experiences the worm appearing in its memories as it devours each one. At the end of each of the target's turns, it takes 88 (16d10) psychic damage. If this damage reduces the target to 0 hit points, the target is stable but loses all its memories permanently. Only a Wish spell can restore the lost memories. Devour Mind ends if the target is reduced to 0 hit points, and the worm appears in an unoccupied space within 60 feet of the target. Devour Mind can also be ended in the following ways:
• As an action, the target mentally duels the worm and makes a DC 20 ability check using Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (its choice). On a success, the worm must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to the check's total or Devour Mind ends.
• Devour Mind ends if the target receives an effect that ends possession, including the Dispel Evil and Good spell.
• If the target takes damage, the worm takes psychic damage equal to half that amount. It must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC equal to the psychic damage taken, or Devour Mind ends.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn, 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The worm can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The worm regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Memory Fragments
legendary.text: The worm expels shards of memories it has devoured. Each creature within 10 feet of the worm must make a DC 18 Intelligence saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

legendary.name: Slither
legendary.text: The worm moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Spawn Parasites (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The worm disgorges a slimy spray of pale, parasitic worms in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be infested with parasitic worms and be poisoned. At the start of the poisoned target's turn, it takes 7 (2d6) necrotic damage plus 7 (2d6) psychic damage as the worms burrow toward its brain. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this damage dies and then rises 24 hours later as a Wormhost Surgeon. The infesting worms are killed by any effect that ends the poisoned condition on the target, or if 10 poison damage or more is dealt to the target.

legendary.name: The White Worm's Lair
legendary.text: The White Worm's lair is a secluded lake and the shores surrounding it. If encountered in its lair, the White Worm has a challenge rating of 20 (25,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the White Worm's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Alien Growth. Strange life grows rampant within 1 mile of the lair. In that region, Gricks, Purple Worms, and Wormhost Surgeons* are common.
Psychic Phantoms. Within 1 mile of the lair, creatures are plagued by horrid nightmares. When a creature sleeps or enters a trance and finishes a long rest in that area, it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or gain no benefit from finishing the rest.
If the White Worm dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Eldritch Devourer of Memories
Born of a forgotten realm beyond the stars, the White Worm is an eldritch horror resembling a humungous, pallid grub that ravenously hungers for memory. Greedily consuming the memories of any beings capable of remembering, its mere existence sends out shockwaves of alien psychic energy. It also serves as the queen of a hivemind colony of infinite wormlike spawn that seek mortal hosts to feast upon and control.
Secret. A specific series of constellations and star paths through the heavens is a deadly poison to the White Worm if it consumes that memory.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 574 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Wicker Man
monster.conditionImmune: charmed, exhaustion, frightened, incapacitated, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, stunned, unconscious

trait.name: Burning Aura
trait.text: At the end of the each of the Wicker Man's turns, each creature of its choice within 15 feet of the Wicker Man takes 5 (1d10) fire damage. Creatures and flammable objects in the area ignite. Until a creature within 5 feet of the fire takes an action to douse the flames, the target takes 2 (1d4) fire damage at the start of each of its turns.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day)
trait.text: If the Wicker Man fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The Wicker Man has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Reactive
trait.text: The Wicker Man can use its reaction on every turn of combat.

trait.name: Soul Motes
trait.text: The Wicker Man has 3 Soul Motes. It gains 1 Soul Mote when a creature that isn't a Construct or Undead dies within 30 feet of it. The Wicker Man can expend Soul Motes to fuel Soul Mote reactions. When the Wicker Man finishes a long rest, its Soul Mote total resets to 3.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The Wicker Man makes two Blazing Slam attacks.

action.name: Blazing Slam
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 15 (2d8 + 6) bludgeoning damage plus 9 (2d8) fire damage.

action.name: Cremating Flame
action.text: The Wicker Man belches a gout of flame in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 35 (10d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this damage is reduced to ash, and its soul is absorbed into the Wicker Man. The creature can't be restored to life by any means until the wicker man is destroyed.

reaction.name: Crushing Blow (Soul Mote)
reaction.text: When the Wicker Man hits with an attack roll, it expends 1 Soul Mote and the hit becomes a critical hit.

reaction.name: Repair (Soul Mote)
reaction.text: When the Wicker Man starts its turn, it expends 1 Soul Mote and regains 20 hit points.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn)
legendary.text: The Wicker Man can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The Wicker Man regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Burning Clutch
legendary.text: One creature within 15 feet of the Wicker Man must succeed on a DC 19 Strength saving throw or be grappled (escape DC 16) from one of two hands, and it is restrained until the grapple ends. At the start of the Wicker Man's turn, the grappled creature takes 10 (3d6) fire damage.

legendary.name: Stride
legendary.text: The Wicker Man moves up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Slam (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The Wicker Man makes one Blazing Slam attack.

legendary.name: The Wicker Man's Lair
legendary.text: The Wicker Man's lair is the hilltop where it is constructed and venerated.

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Wicker Man's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Biting Flames. Within 1 mile of the lair, when fire damage is dealt to a creature outside the Wicker Man's dedicated cult that has resistance or immunity to fire damage, the creature takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage.
Endless Praise. Within 1 mile of the lair, cultists of the Wicker Man who sing their cult's praises or hymns can be heard up to 300 feet away, even through a Silence spell.
If the Wicker Man dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Blazing Sacrifcial Colossus
The ultimate expression of a cult's dedication to its patron is the Wicker Man. This massive Construct is woven from wicker and bound with wire and iron bands in the shape of a great bipedal effigy. The chest cavity and limbs are hollow where sacrifces can be secured, with the wicker skin forming cage-like bars and gaps. Celebrated and protected with a cult's very existence, the Wicker Man stands silent and impassive until it is ignited while filled with living sacrifices. Then it comes to life and seeks to consume any nonbelievers—and sometimes even the faithful.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 576 (Third Party)

monster.name: The Wild Titan

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (4/Day or 5/Day in Lair)
trait.text: If the titan fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The titan has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Runic Sever
trait.text: If the titan starts its turn within 50 feet of a stone at least 10 feet high and 10 feet wide that is carved with the glyph representing its forgotten creator, the titan takes 50 necrotic damage and is poisoned until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Siege Monster
trait.text: The titan deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The titan makes two Massive Fist attacks. It can replace one or both attacks with a use of Thrown Boulder.

action.name: Massive Fist
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +17 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (4d8 + 10) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 20) from one of two fists and restrained until the grapple ends.

action.name: Thrown Boulder
action.text: The titan hurls a boulder at a point it can see within 150 feet of itself. Each creature in a 10-foot radius sphere centered on that point must make a DC 25 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Wild Roar (Recharge 5-6)
action.text: Each of the titan's enemies with 100 feet of it must succeed on a DC 19 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed until the start of the titan's next turn. While charmed in this way, the creature must use its action to make its most powerful melee or ranged attacks against the nearest creature it can see.

legendary.name: Legendary Action (3/Turn, 4/Turn in Lair)
legendary.text: The titan can take 3 legendary actions (4 in Lair), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The titan regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

legendary.name: Arm Sweep
legendary.text: Each creature in a 20-foot cone must make a DC 25 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 9 (2d8) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

legendary.name: Stomping Stride
legendary.text: The titan moves up to its speed without provoking opportunity attacks. It can enter a Huge or smaller creature's space, and the creature is pushed to nearest unoccupied space.

legendary.name: Crushing Hurl (Costs 2 Actions)
legendary.text: The titan hurls an object it is holding, a creature it is grappling, or a Large clod of earth to a space within 100 feet. Each creature in the projectile's space, including a hurled creature, must make a DC 25 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 19 (2d8 + 10) bludgeoning damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

legendary.name: The Wild Titan's Lair
legendary.text: The Wild Titan's lair is a hollowed-out peak in the mountain range the creature stalks. If encountered in its lair, the Wild Titan has a challenge rating of 22 (41,000 XP).

legendary.name: Regional Effects
legendary.text:
The region containing the Wild Titan's lair is changed by its presence, creating the following effects:
Whiteout. Within 1 mile of the lair, there is a 75 percent chance every day of heavy precipitation.
Wild Hunger. Within 1 mile of the lair, creatures always feel ravenously hungry, no matter how much they've eaten. A creature that finishes a long rest in this area must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or gain 1 level of exhaustion. A creature that doesn't require food succeeds automatically.
If the Wild Titan dies or moves its lair elsewhere, these effects end immediately.

monster.description:
Ferocious Giant of the Wilderness
Wandering the most inhospitable mountain ranges, the Wild Titan is the wilderness made manifest, created long ago by a forgotten will for a purpose that none know. Favoring solitude in the most remote places of the world, when it encounters settlements and "civilized" people, it wants only to turn them to primal savagery or crush them beneath its immense bulk.
Secret. The name of the Wild Titan's forgotten creator is its undoing. Recovering the lost name and carving its glyph form into a stone creates a zone that severs the titan's connection to the wild.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 578 (Third Party)

monster.name: Corn Dolly
monster.languages: understands Common but doesn't speak it

trait.name: Husk Body
trait.text: The corn dolly can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing.

trait.name: Uneasy Visage
trait.text: The strange, childlike inked face unnerves onlookers. While a creature is holding the corn dolly, creatures within 10 feet of it have disadvantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks made against the creature.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Corn Dolly.

action.name: Husk Cut
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 1 slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) psychic damage.

action.name: Blank Stare
action.text: The corn dolly stares at one creature it can see within 30 feet of itself. The target must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

action.name: Sowing Dread (2/Day)
action.text: The corn dolly radiates dread in a 15-foot cone. Each creature of the corn dolly's choice in that area must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened until the end of its next turn.

reaction.name: Silent Protector
reaction.text: When a creature within 60 feet of the corn dolly fails a saving throw against an effect that doesn't deal damage, the corn dolly suffers the effects of the failure instead of the creature.

monster.description:
Grain Husk Companion (Curved blade plated in gold)
Crafted from dried husks and bound with twine, corn dollies' delicate forms are stitched together with rustic care. Their faces are ink-brushed with simple, childlike expressions—smiling mouths that curve a bit too wide and hollow eyes that seem to stare far beyond the physical world. The Constructs are spirits of deceased Humanoids drawn to the hollow corn husk shell, seeking refuge. These spirits retain faint traces of who they once were—a protective instinct entwined with a simmering fury. Once animated, the dolls reflect this dual nature: acting as silent guardians one moment and vengeful specters the next.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 581 (Third Party)

monster.name: Crooked Hare
monster.skill: Deception +5, History +2, Sleight of Hand +5, Stealth +5

trait.name: Trackless Step
trait.text: The crooked hare leaves no tracks, or it leaves the tracks of a Large or smaller creature of its choice. If it isn't incapacitated, it can share the effects of this trait with another creature within 5 feet of itself (no action required).

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Crooked Hare.

action.name: Twisting Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 7 (2d6) poison damage, and if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 11 Charisma saving throw or become cursed for 24 hours. The cursed creature's limbs sporadically twist and move in unnatural ways, giving the creature disadvantage on attack rolls and on ability checks that rely on Charisma.

monster.description:
Crooked Trickster (Anything stolen or gained by trickery)
Crooked hares look like rabbits at first glance, but their true nature is given away by the antlers and wings they grow and the cunning gleam in their eyes. They are mischievous tricksters that delight in stealing things from unsuspecting travelers and hiding them in amusing, slightly dangerous, or just unlikely places. If roused to anger or cornered, crooked hares deliver a bite that carries an unsettling—if minor—curse. Despite their troublesome nature, crooked hares might serve as companions for woods-crafty folk who have a good sense of humor.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 582 (Third Party)

monster.name: Devil's Glove

trait.name: Scent of Death
trait.text: Creatures that can smell have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to find the devil's glove.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The devil's glove can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Devil's Glove.

action.name: Tendril
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) necrotic damage, and the devil's glove attaches to the target. While attached, the devil's glove can't make Tendril attacks against other targets, and if the target is a creature, the target is poisoned until the start of its next turn if it can smell the devil's glove. The devil's glove can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the devil's glove as an action.

action.name: Decaying Bloom (2/Day)
action.text: The devil's glove releases a cloud of spores. Each creature within 10 feet of the devil's glove must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 9 (2d8) necrotic damage and can't regain hit points until the start of the devil's glove's next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

action.name: Symbiosis (Bonus Action)
action.text: The devil's glove forms a magical bond with a willing creature it touches. The bonded creature doesn't need to eat or drink, and the creature has advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the poisoned condition. The bond ends when the devil's glove dies, the creature dies, the devil's glove ends the bond (no action required), or the devil's glove uses this bonus action again.

monster.description:
Five-Fingered Fungus (Rare wood chips)
Comprised of five twitching, blood-red tendrils lined with black slime, the devil's glove fungus feeds voraciously of decaying matter, unwary insects, and small creatures. Resembling a long-fngered hand, they grow from bulbous roots that look like leathery, white eggs and constantly emit an overpowering scent of death and rot. Despite their unpleasant aura, these fungus creatures can form beneficial relationships with others, providing nourishment and resilience in exchange for protection and a ready supply of decaying material to feed upon.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 583 (Third Party)

monster.name: Dingpot

trait.name: Boil and Bubble
trait.text: While the dingpot isn't incapacitated, it can provide enough heat to boil water within its vessel.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Dingpot.

action.name: Steam Spray
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (2d4 + 2) fire damage, and the target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the start of the dingpot's next turn. Being underwater doesn't grant resistance to this fire damage.

action.name: Brew (1/Day)
action.text:
The dingpot produces a magical elixir within its vessel. The elixir remains potent for 24 hours. It can choose from the following brews:
Healing Draught. As a bonus action, a creature can drink the elixir to regain 4 (1d6 + 1) hit points.
Lucky Draught. As a bonus action, a creature can drink the elixir to gain startling luck for 10 minutes. When the creature fails an ability check or a saving throw, it can reroll the d20 and use the new roll. Once this causes a roll to succeed, the effects of the elixir end early.

monster.description:
Jovial Cauldron Helper (Spices and rose quartz)
Dingpots are animated objects created to assist in magical and alchemical research—or simply to help in the kitchen. Often cheerful, dingpots delight in serving up delicious meals, soothing tea, and the occasional magical draught.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 584 (Third Party)

monster.name: Ghost Crawler

trait.name: Ephemeral Body
trait.text: The crawler can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without squeezing.

trait.name: Seer's Strand
trait.text: The crawler can see and hear through the space of its Ether Webs as if it were in that space.

trait.name: Spider Climb
trait.text: The crawler can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Web Sense
trait.text: While in contact with a web, the crawler knows the exact location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

trait.name: Web Walker
trait.text: The crawler ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Ghost Crawler.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The crawler makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage, and if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the start of the crawler's next turn.

action.name: Ether Webs (2/Day)
action.text: The crawler fills a 5-foot cube with invisible, ephemeral webs for 8 hours. If the web isn't anchored between two solid masses (such as walls or trees) or layered across a floor, wall, or ceiling, the web collapses on itself and vanishes. When a creature touches the webbing, the crawler knows the location of the creature as long as they are on the same plane of existence.

monster.description:
Spirit Spider (Silk threads soaked in wine)
Silent and nearly invisible, ghost crawlers skitter between the darkest corners, hiding and watching. Their diminutive, ghostly, spider-like bodies are ill-defned and as translucent as a wisp of smoke, while their legs are long, uneven, and jarringly solid. The webs they spin are ephemeral and ghastly, allowing the crawlers to sense through them regardless of the distance.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 585 (Third Party)

monster.name: Goatstealer

trait.name: Pack Tactics
trait.text: The goatstealer has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the goatstealer's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Stench
trait.text: Any creature that starts its turn within 5 feet of the goatstealer must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature is immune to the Stench of all goatstealers for 1 hour.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Goatstealer.

action.name: Bite
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) poison damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 12). Until the grapple ends, at the start of each of the goatstealer's turns, the target takes 3 (1d6) poison damage.

monster.description:
Hungry Livestock Snatcher (Incense and spoiled meat)
The hairless, reeking goatstealers are vicious predators and scavengers that earn their name from their habit of preying upon livestock. They latch onto prey with wicked, needle-like teeth, delivering lethal venom and devouring creatures far larger than its wiry frame would suggest is possible. Especially dangerous in packs, the carrion-yet-peaty stench of goatstealers is the stuf of legend.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 586 (Third Party)

monster.name: Hemonculus

trait.name: Blood Sense
trait.text: The hemonculus knows the location of any creature within 60 feet of it that has half its hit points or fewer, unless the creature is a Construct or Undead.

trait.name: Flyby
trait.text: The hemonculus doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
trait.text: The hemonculus has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Hemonculus.

action.name: Claw
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) necrotic damage, and the hemonculus attaches to the target. While attached, the hemonculus automatically hits the target with its Claw attacks. The hemonculus can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement.
