The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the hemonculus as an action.

action.name: Exsanguinate (1/Day)
action.text: The hemonculus magically draws blood from nearby creatures. Each creature of the hemonculus' choice within 15 feet of the hemonculus must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes 10 (3d6) necrotic damage, and it can't regain hit points until the start of the hemonculus's next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. If the hemonculus is attached to a creature, the hemonculus regains hit points equal to the necrotic damage taken by that target.

monster.description:
Sanguine Servitor (Fresh blood and gem dust)
Hemonculus are servitors created by a blood magic ritual that binds a fiendish spirit into a blood-formed body. For the cost of a small sacrifce in blood (the creator's own or otherwise), the creatures serve as spies and assist in procuring further blood for magical use.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 587 (Third Party)

monster.name: Magpie of Omens

trait.name: Flyby
trait.text: The magpie doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Magpie of Omens.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The magpie makes two Beak attacks.

action.name: Beak
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) radiant damage.

action.name: Bonded Voice (Bonus Action)
action.text: The magpie can form or dissolve a magical bond with a willing creature within 5 feet. While the bond lasts, the bonded creature can speak through the magpie using the creature's own voice and language, but with a birdlike lilt. The bond ends when the creature dies or if the magpie creates a new bond.

reaction.name: One for Sorrow (1/Day)
reaction.text: When a creature within 30 feet of the magpie makes an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw with advantage, the target rolls 1d6 and subtracts it from the total.

reaction.name: Two for Joy (2/Day)
reaction.text: When the magpie or another creature within 30 feet of it makes an ability check or a saving throw, the target rolls 1d4 and adds it to the total

monster.description:
Bird of Fate and Fortune (Crystal or gemstone shards)
To those who respect the Old Ways, this bird of black and white brings tidings both fair and foul. Although the omens the magpie delivers can bring joy and grief in equal measure, they are always trustworthy.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 588 (Third Party)

monster.name: Moonshine
monster.resist: acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, thunder; bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing from nonmagical attacks
monster.conditionImmune: exhaustion, grappled, paralyzed, petrifed, poisoned, prone, restrained, unconscious

trait.name: Detect Life
trait.text: The moonshine magically senses the direction of creatures up to 60 feet away that aren't Constructs or Undead.

trait.name: Ephemeral
trait.text: The moonshine can't wear or carry anything.

trait.name: Illumination
trait.text: The moonshine sheds bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
trait.text: The moonshine can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Moonshine.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The moonshine makes two Gloomy Touch attacks.

action.name: Gloomy Touch
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) psychic damage.

action.name: Siphon Life (2/Day)
action.text: The moonshine magically targets a creature that isn't a Construct or Undead that it can see within 5 feet of itself. The target must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of the moonshine's next turn, and the moonshine or a creature of its choice within 30 feet of it regains 3 (1d6) hit points.

monster.description:
Lingering Soul Guide (Broken glass and powdered onyx)
When a person dies with unfinished business, their soul can remain shackled to the living world as an Undead spirit. Usually such spirits rest and move on when their business is concluded, but some remain as companions, guides, and helpers for the living. Moonshines are spirits who resolved their emotional tethers but have chosen to linger on. They often take a liking to a living person and choose to help them through their life's journey.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 589 (Third Party)

monster.name: Nameless Thing

trait.name: Flyby
trait.text: The nameless thing doesn't provoke an opportunity attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Nameless Thing.

action.name: Psychic Strike
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d12 + 3) psychic damage, and if the target is a creature, it is frightened until the start of the nameless thing's next turn.

action.name: Invisibility
action.text: The nameless thing casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability. When it casts the spell, the nameless thing can extend the effect of the spell to a creature it is touching. The spell ends early if either creature breaks the spell on itself or if the nameless thing stops touching the other creature. Once the nameless thing extends the spell in this way, it can't do so again until it finishes a long rest.

monster.description:
Unknowable Lifeform from Beyond (Meteorite fragments)
Nameless things are alien minds bound into physical vessels, most often diminutive animals, objects, or pieces of monsters. These creatures can creep into the world when outer infuence seeps into existence but are usually deliberately invited.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 590 (Third Party)

monster.name: Ravencraft

trait.name: Undead Fortitude
trait.text: If damage reduces the ravencraft to 0 hit points, it makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken) unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a successful save, the ravencraft drops to 1 hit point instead.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Ravencraft.

action.name: Beak
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) necrotic damage.

action.name: Harbinger of Doom (2/Day)
action.text: The ravencraft lets out a wail of anguish commingling with the croaking of ravens. One creature within 60 feet of it must make a DC 11 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 5 (1d10) psychic damage, and it is frightened until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.

reaction.name: Death Veil
reaction.text: When a creature within 30 feet of the ravencraft makes a death saving throw, the creature has advantage on the save.

monster.description:
Undead Avian Amalgamation (Silver wire)
Ravencrafts are a grotesque fusion of feathers, sinew, and bone. Their tiny form is a contorted and twisted nightmare carelessly sewn together into the mockery of a bird. The mere presence of a ravencraft is an afront to natural death and can keep further death at bay, or they can chill the hearts of their foes with cries of torment from beyond the grave.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 591 (Third Party)

monster.name: Salmon of Knowledge
monster.speed: 5 ft., fy 30 ft. (hover; Orb of Water only), swim 30 ft.
monster.languages: understands all but can't speak, Telepathy 60 ft.

trait.name: Orb of Water
trait.text: The salmon is surrounded by a sphere of flowing water that moves with it. While inside of the sphere, the salmon has resistance to fire damage. A creature that grapples the salmon can choose to pull it out of the orb of water. The orb of water moves up to 20 feet toward the salmon at the start of each of the salmon's turns. If it enters the salmon's space, the grapple ends on the salmon.

trait.name: Water Breathing
trait.text: The salmon can breathe only underwater.

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Salmon of Knowledge.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The salmon makes two Water Spray attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Prophetic Guidance.

action.name: Water Spray
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) cold damage.

action.name: Prophetic Guidance (2/Day)
action.text: A creature the salmon can see within 30 feet gains a flash of foresight. Once in the next minute, when the creature fails an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it can roll 1d4 and add it to the total, potentially turning the failure into a success.

reaction.name: Echo from the Current
reaction.text: When a creature within 5 feet of the salmon makes an Intelligence or Wisdom check, the creature can use the salmon's Wisdom modifier (+4) in place of its own ability modifier for the check.

monster.description:
Elemental Font of Wisdom (Crushed opal)
Legends speak of the salmon of knowledge—shimmering fish said to embody the wisdom of the ages. Their scales gleam with an iridescent light, and they are forever enveloped in a sphere of gently fowing water that defes gravity. This magical orb is said to carry the essence of the sacred Current of Wisdom—where knowledge of all time and space fows through the rivers of the cosmos.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 592 (Third Party)
monster.environment: coastal, planar (elemental plane of water), underwater

monster.name: Witchwoven

trait.name: Find Familiar
trait.text: With the DM's permission, a character who casts the 'Find Familiar' spell can choose to summon a Witchwoven.

action.name: Cursed Scratch
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage plus 3 (1d6) necrotic damage, and if the target is a creature, it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of the witchwoven's next turn.

reaction.name: Protective Bond
reaction.text: When a creature the witchwoven can see within 60 feet of it takes damage, the creature gains resistance to the triggering damage, and the witchwoven takes 10 (3d6) force damage.

monster.description:
Helper Made of Twine and Twigs (Ruby wrapped in a wicker cage)
Witchwoven are little dolls or efgies made of sticks, twine, and other simple materials, then imbued with life. Some happen naturally in places sufused with old nature magic, but most are created to serve as familiars by spellcasters knowledgeable in the Old Ways.
Source: The Crooked Moon p. 593 (Third Party)
